34\u2192\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192class AuthorAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):\n    37\u2192    ordering = [\"id\"]\n    38\u2192    search_fields = [\"id\"]\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192class AuthorshipInline(admin.TabularInline):\n    42\u2192    model = Authorship\n    43\u2192    autocomplete_fields = [\"author\"]\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192class BookAdmin(admin.ModelAdmin):\n    47\u2192    inlines = [AuthorshipInline]\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192site = admin.AdminSite(name=\"autocomplete_admin\")\n    51\u2192site.register(Question, QuestionAdmin)\n    52\u2192site.register(Answer, AnswerAdmin)\n    53\u2192site.register(Author, AuthorAdmin)\n    54\u2192site.register(Book, BookAdmin)\n    55\u2192site.register(Employee, search_fields=[\"name\"])\n    56\u2192site.register(WorkHour, autocomplete_fields=[\"employee\"])\n    57\u2192site.register(Manager, search_fields=[\"name\"])\n    58\u2192site.register(Bonus, autocomplete_fields=[\"recipient\"])\n    59\u2192site.register(PKChild, search_fields=[\"name\"])\n    60\u2192site.register(Toy, autocomplete_fields=[\"child\"])\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192@contextmanager\n    64\u2192def model_admin(model, model_admin, admin_site=site):\n    65\u2192    try:\n    66\u2192        org_admin = admin_site.get_model_admin(model)\n    67\u2192    except NotRegistered:\n    68\u2192        org_admin = None\n    69\u2192    else:\n    70\u2192        admin_site.unregister(model)\n    71\u2192    admin_site.register(model, model_admin)\n    72\u2192    try:\n    73\u2192        yield\n    74\u2192    finally:\n    75\u2192        if org_admin:\n    76\u2192            admin_site._registry[model] = org_admin\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192class AutocompleteJsonViewTests(AdminViewBasicTestCase):\n    80\u2192    as_view_args = {\"admin_site\": site}\n    81\u2192    opts = {\n    82\u2192        \"app_label\": Answer._meta.app_label,\n    83\u2192        \"model_name\": Answer._meta.model_name,\n    84\u2192        \"field_name\": \"question\",\n    85\u2192    }\n    86\u2192    factory = RequestFactory()\n    87\u2192    url = reverse_lazy(\"autocomplete_admin:autocomplete\")\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192    @classmethod\n    90\u2192    def setUpTestData(cls):\n    91\u2192        cls.user = User.objects.create_user(\n    92\u2192            username=\"user\",\n    93\u2192            password=\"secret\",\n    94\u2192            email=\"user@example.com\",\n    95\u2192            is_staff=True,\n    96\u2192        )\n    97\u2192        super().setUpTestData()\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192    def test_success(self):\n   100\u2192        q = Question.objects.create(question=\"Is this a question?\")\n   101\u2192        request = self.factory.get(self.url, {\"term\": \"is\", **self.opts})\n   102\u2192        request.user = self.superuser\n   103\u2192        response = AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   104\u2192        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)\n   105\u2192        data = json.loads(response.text)\n   106\u2192        self.assertEqual(\n   107\u2192            data,\n   108\u2192            {\n   109\u2192                \"results\": [{\"id\": str(q.pk), \"text\": q.question}],\n   110\u2192                \"pagination\": {\"more\": False},\n   111\u2192            },\n   112\u2192        )\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192    def test_custom_to_field(self):\n   115\u2192        q = Question.objects.create(question=\"Is this a question?\")\n   116\u2192        request = self.factory.get(\n   117\u2192            self.url,\n   118\u2192            {\"term\": \"is\", **self.opts, \"field_name\": \"question_with_to_field\"},\n   119\u2192        )\n   120\u2192        request.user = self.superuser\n   121\u2192        response = AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   122\u2192        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)\n   123\u2192        data = json.loads(response.text)\n   124\u2192        self.assertEqual(\n   125\u2192            data,\n   126\u2192            {\n   127\u2192                \"results\": [{\"id\": str(q.uuid), \"text\": q.question}],\n   128\u2192                \"pagination\": {\"more\": False},\n   129\u2192            },\n   130\u2192        )\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192    def test_custom_to_field_permission_denied(self):\n   133\u2192        Question.objects.create(question=\"Is this a question?\")\n   134\u2192        request = self.factory.get(\n   135\u2192            self.url,\n   136\u2192            {\"term\": \"is\", **self.opts, \"field_name\": \"question_with_to_field\"},\n   137\u2192        )\n   138\u2192        request.user = self.user\n   139\u2192        with self.assertRaises(PermissionDenied):\n   140\u2192            AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192    def test_custom_to_field_custom_pk(self):\n   143\u2192        q = Question.objects.create(question=\"Is this a question?\")\n   144\u2192        opts = {\n   145\u2192            \"app_label\": Question._meta.app_label,\n   146\u2192            \"model_name\": Question._meta.model_name,\n   147\u2192            \"field_name\": \"related_questions\",\n   148\u2192        }\n   149\u2192        request = self.factory.get(self.url, {\"term\": \"is\", **opts})\n   150\u2192        request.user = self.superuser\n   151\u2192        response = AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   152\u2192        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)\n   153\u2192        data = json.loads(response.text)\n   154\u2192        self.assertEqual(\n   155\u2192            data,\n   156\u2192            {\n   157\u2192                \"results\": [{\"id\": str(q.big_id), \"text\": q.question}],\n   158\u2192                \"pagination\": {\"more\": False},\n   159\u2192            },\n   160\u2192        )\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192    def test_to_field_resolution_with_mti(self):\n   163\u2192        \"\"\"\n   164\u2192        to_field resolution should correctly resolve for target models using\n   165\u2192        MTI. Tests for single and multi-level cases.\n   166\u2192        \"\"\"\n   167\u2192        tests = [\n   168\u2192            (Employee, WorkHour, \"employee\"),\n   169\u2192            (Manager, Bonus, \"recipient\"),\n   170\u2192        ]\n   171\u2192        for Target, Remote, related_name in tests:\n   172\u2192            with self.subTest(\n   173\u2192                target_model=Target, remote_model=Remote, related_name=related_name\n   174\u2192            ):\n   175\u2192                o = Target.objects.create(\n   176\u2192                    name=\"Frida Kahlo\", gender=2, code=\"painter\", alive=False\n   177\u2192                )\n   178\u2192                opts = {\n   179\u2192                    \"app_label\": Remote._meta.app_label,\n   180\u2192                    \"model_name\": Remote._meta.model_name,\n   181\u2192                    \"field_name\": related_name,\n   182\u2192                }\n   183\u2192                request = self.factory.get(self.url, {\"term\": \"frida\", **opts})\n   184\u2192                request.user = self.superuser\n   185\u2192                response = AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   186\u2192                self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)\n   187\u2192                data = json.loads(response.text)\n   188\u2192                self.assertEqual(\n   189\u2192                    data,\n   190\u2192                    {\n   191\u2192                        \"results\": [{\"id\": str(o.pk), \"text\": o.name}],\n   192\u2192                        \"pagination\": {\"more\": False},\n   193\u2192                    },\n   194\u2192                )\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192    def test_to_field_resolution_with_fk_pk(self):\n   197\u2192        p = Parent.objects.create(name=\"Bertie\")\n   198\u2192        c = PKChild.objects.create(parent=p, name=\"Anna\")\n   199\u2192        opts = {\n   200\u2192            \"app_label\": Toy._meta.app_label,\n   201\u2192            \"model_name\": Toy._meta.model_name,\n   202\u2192            \"field_name\": \"child\",\n   203\u2192        }\n   204\u2192        request = self.factory.get(self.url, {\"term\": \"anna\", **opts})\n   205\u2192        request.user = self.superuser\n   206\u2192        response = AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   207\u2192        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)\n   208\u2192        data = json.loads(response.text)\n   209\u2192        self.assertEqual(\n   210\u2192            data,\n   211\u2192            {\n   212\u2192                \"results\": [{\"id\": str(c.pk), \"text\": c.name}],\n   213\u2192                \"pagination\": {\"more\": False},\n   214\u2192            },\n   215\u2192        )\n   216\u2192\n   217\u2192    def test_field_does_not_exist(self):\n   218\u2192        request = self.factory.get(\n   219\u2192            self.url, {\"term\": \"is\", **self.opts, \"field_name\": \"does_not_exist\"}\n   220\u2192        )\n   221\u2192        request.user = self.superuser\n   222\u2192        with self.assertRaises(PermissionDenied):\n   223\u2192            AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   224\u2192\n   225\u2192    def test_field_no_related_field(self):\n   226\u2192        request = self.factory.get(\n   227\u2192            self.url, {\"term\": \"is\", **self.opts, \"field_name\": \"answer\"}\n   228\u2192        )\n   229\u2192        request.user = self.superuser\n   230\u2192        with self.assertRaises(PermissionDenied):\n   231\u2192            AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   232\u2192\n   233\u2192    def test_field_does_not_allowed(self):\n   234\u2192        request = self.factory.get(\n   235\u2192            self.url, {\"term\": \"is\", **self.opts, \"field_name\": \"related_questions\"}\n   236\u2192        )\n   237\u2192        request.user = self.superuser\n   238\u2192        with self.assertRaises(PermissionDenied):\n   239\u2192            AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   240\u2192\n   241\u2192    def test_limit_choices_to(self):\n   242\u2192        # Answer.question_with_to_field defines limit_choices_to to \"those not\n   243\u2192        # starting with 'not'\".\n   244\u2192        q = Question.objects.create(question=\"Is this a question?\")\n   245\u2192        Question.objects.create(question=\"Not a question.\")\n   246\u2192        request = self.factory.get(\n   247\u2192            self.url,\n   248\u2192            {\"term\": \"is\", **self.opts, \"field_name\": \"question_with_to_field\"},\n   249\u2192        )\n   250\u2192        request.user = self.superuser\n   251\u2192        response = AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   252\u2192        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)\n   253\u2192        data = json.loads(response.text)\n   254\u2192        self.assertEqual(\n   255\u2192            data,\n   256\u2192            {\n   257\u2192                \"results\": [{\"id\": str(q.uuid), \"text\": q.question}],\n   258\u2192                \"pagination\": {\"more\": False},\n   259\u2192            },\n   260\u2192        )\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192    def test_must_be_logged_in(self):\n   263\u2192        response = self.client.get(self.url, {\"term\": \"\", **self.opts})\n   264\u2192        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)\n   265\u2192        self.client.logout()\n   266\u2192        response = self.client.get(self.url, {\"term\": \"\", **self.opts})\n   267\u2192        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192    def test_has_view_or_change_permission_required(self):\n   270\u2192        \"\"\"\n   271\u2192        Users require the change permission for the related model to the\n   272\u2192        autocomplete view for it.\n   273\u2192        \"\"\"\n   274\u2192        request = self.factory.get(self.url, {\"term\": \"is\", **self.opts})\n   275\u2192        request.user = self.user\n   276\u2192        with self.assertRaises(PermissionDenied):\n   277\u2192            AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   278\u2192        for permission in (\"view\", \"change\"):\n   279\u2192            with self.subTest(permission=permission):\n   280\u2192                self.user.user_permissions.clear()\n   281\u2192                p = Permission.objects.get(\n   282\u2192                    content_type=ContentType.objects.get_for_model(Question),\n   283\u2192                    codename=\"%s_question\" % permission,\n   284\u2192                )\n   285\u2192                self.user.user_permissions.add(p)\n   286\u2192                request.user = User.objects.get(pk=self.user.pk)\n   287\u2192                response = AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   288\u2192                self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)\n   289\u2192\n   290\u2192    def test_search_use_distinct(self):\n   291\u2192        \"\"\"\n   292\u2192        Searching across model relations use QuerySet.distinct() to avoid\n   293\u2192        duplicates.\n   294\u2192        \"\"\"\n   295\u2192        q1 = Question.objects.create(question=\"question 1\")\n   296\u2192        q2 = Question.objects.create(question=\"question 2\")\n   297\u2192        q2.related_questions.add(q1)\n   298\u2192        q3 = Question.objects.create(question=\"question 3\")\n   299\u2192        q3.related_questions.add(q1)\n   300\u2192        request = self.factory.get(self.url, {\"term\": \"question\", **self.opts})\n   301\u2192        request.user = self.superuser\n   302\u2192\n   303\u2192        class DistinctQuestionAdmin(QuestionAdmin):\n   304\u2192            search_fields = [\"related_questions__question\", \"question\"]\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192        with model_admin(Question, DistinctQuestionAdmin):\n   307\u2192            response = AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   308\u2192        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)\n   309\u2192        data = json.loads(response.text)\n   310\u2192        self.assertEqual(len(data[\"results\"]), 3)\n   311\u2192\n   312\u2192    def test_missing_search_fields(self):\n   313\u2192        class EmptySearchAdmin(QuestionAdmin):\n   314\u2192            search_fields = []\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192        with model_admin(Question, EmptySearchAdmin):\n   317\u2192            msg = \"EmptySearchAdmin must have search_fields for the autocomplete_view.\"\n   318\u2192            with self.assertRaisesMessage(Http404, msg):\n   319\u2192                site.autocomplete_view(\n   320\u2192                    self.factory.get(self.url, {\"term\": \"\", **self.opts})\n   321\u2192                )\n   322\u2192\n   323\u2192    def test_get_paginator(self):\n   324\u2192        \"\"\"Search results are paginated.\"\"\"\n   325\u2192\n   326\u2192        class PKOrderingQuestionAdmin(QuestionAdmin):\n   327\u2192            ordering = [\"pk\"]\n   328\u2192\n   329\u2192        Question.objects.bulk_create(\n   330\u2192            Question(question=str(i)) for i in range(PAGINATOR_SIZE + 10)\n   331\u2192        )\n   332\u2192        # The first page of results.\n   333\u2192        request = self.factory.get(self.url, {\"term\": \"\", **self.opts})\n   334\u2192        request.user = self.superuser\n   335\u2192        with model_admin(Question, PKOrderingQuestionAdmin):\n   336\u2192            response = AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   337\u2192        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)\n   338\u2192        data = json.loads(response.text)\n   339\u2192        self.assertEqual(\n   340\u2192            data,\n   341\u2192            {\n   342\u2192                \"results\": [\n   343\u2192                    {\"id\": str(q.pk), \"text\": q.question}\n   344\u2192                    for q in Question.objects.all()[:PAGINATOR_SIZE]\n   345\u2192                ],\n   346\u2192                \"pagination\": {\"more\": True},\n   347\u2192            },\n   348\u2192        )\n   349\u2192        # The second page of results.\n   350\u2192        request = self.factory.get(self.url, {\"term\": \"\", \"page\": \"2\", **self.opts})\n   351\u2192        request.user = self.superuser\n   352\u2192        with model_admin(Question, PKOrderingQuestionAdmin):\n   353\u2192            response = AutocompleteJsonView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(request)\n   354\u2192        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)\n   355\u2192        data = json.loads(response.text)\n   356\u2192        self.assertEqual(\n   357\u2192            data,\n   358\u2192            {\n   359\u2192                \"results\": [\n   360\u2192                    {\"id\": str(q.pk), \"text\": q.question}\n   361\u2192                    for q in Question.objects.all()[PAGINATOR_SIZE:]\n   362\u2192                ],\n   363\u2192                \"pagination\": {\"more\": False},\n   364\u2192            },\n   365\u2192        )\n   366\u2192\n   367\u2192    def test_serialize_result(self):\n   368\u2192        class AutocompleteJsonSerializeResultView(AutocompleteJsonView):\n   369\u2192            def serialize_result(self, obj, to_field_name):\n   370\u2192                return {\n   371\u2192                    **super().serialize_result(obj, to_field_name),\n   372\u2192                    \"posted\": str(obj.posted),\n   373\u2192                }\n   374\u2192\n   375\u2192        Question.objects.create(question=\"Question 1\", posted=datetime.date(2021, 8, 9))\n   376\u2192        Question.objects.create(question=\"Question 2\", posted=datetime.date(2021, 8, 7))\n   377\u2192        request = self.factory.get(self.url, {\"term\": \"question\", **self.opts})\n   378\u2192        request.user = self.superuser\n   379\u2192        response = AutocompleteJsonSerializeResultView.as_view(**self.as_view_args)(\n   380\u2192            request\n   381\u2192        )\n   382\u2192        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)\n   383\u2192        data = json.loads(response.text)\n   384\u2192        self.assertEqual(\n   385\u2192            data,\n   386\u2192            {\n   387\u2192                \"results\": [\n   388\u2192                    {\"id\": str(q.pk), \"text\": q.question, \"posted\": str(q.posted)}\n   389\u2192                    for q in Question.objects.order_by(\"-posted\")\n   390\u2192                ],\n   391\u2192                \"pagination\": {\"more\": False},\n   392\u2192            },\n   393\u2192        )\n   394\u2192\n   395\u2192\n   396\u2192@override_settings(ROOT_URLCONF=\"admin_views.urls\")\n   397\u2192class SeleniumTests(AdminSeleniumTestCase):\n   398\u2192    available_apps = [\"admin_views\"] + AdminSeleniumTestCase.available_apps\n   399\u2192\n   400\u2192    def setUp(self):\n   401\u2192        self.superuser = User.objects.create_superuser(\n   402\u2192            username=\"super\",\n   403\u2192            password=\"secret\",\n   404\u2192            email=\"super@example.com\",\n   405\u2192        )\n   406\u2192        self.admin_login(\n   407\u2192            username=\"super\",\n   408\u2192            password=\"secret\",\n   409\u2192            login_url=reverse(\"autocomplete_admin:index\"),\n   410\u2192        )\n   411\u2192\n   412\u2192    @contextmanager\n   413\u2192    def select2_ajax_wait(self, timeout=10):\n   414\u2192        from selenium.common.exceptions import NoSuchElementException\n   415\u2192        from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By\n   416\u2192        from selenium.webdriver.support import expected_conditions as ec\n   417\u2192\n   418\u2192        yield\n   419\u2192        with self.disable_implicit_wait():\n   420\u2192            try:\n   421\u2192                loading_element = self.selenium.find_element(\n   422\u2192                    By.CSS_SELECTOR, \"li.select2-results__option.loading-results\"\n   423\u2192                )\n   424\u2192            except NoSuchElementException:\n   425\u2192                pass\n   426\u2192            else:\n   427\u2192                self.wait_until(ec.staleness_of(loading_element), timeout=timeout)\n   428\u2192\n   429\u2192    def test_select(self):\n   430\u2192        from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By\n   431\u2192        from selenium.webdriver.common.keys import Keys\n   432\u2192        from selenium.webdriver.support.ui import Select\n   433\u2192\n   434\u2192        self.selenium.get(\n   435\u2192            self.live_server_url + reverse(\"autocomplete_admin:admin_views_answer_add\")\n   436\u2192        )\n   437\u2192        elem = self.selenium.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, \".select2-selection\")\n   438\u2192        with self.select2_ajax_wait():\n   439\u2192            elem.click()  # Open the autocomplete dropdown.\n   440\u2192        results = self.selenium.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, \".select2-results\")\n   441\u2192        self.assertTrue(results.is_displayed())\n   442\u2192        option = self.selenium.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, \".select2-results__option\")\n   443\u2192        self.assertEqual(option.text, \"No results found\")\n   444\u2192        with self.select2_ajax_wait():\n   445\u2192            elem.click()  # Close the autocomplete dropdown.\n   446\u2192        q1 = Question.objects.create(question=\"Who am I?\")\n   447\u2192        Question.objects.bulk_create(\n   448\u2192            Question(question=str(i)) for i in range(PAGINATOR_SIZE + 10)\n   449\u2192        )\n   450\u2192        with self.select2_ajax_wait():\n   451\u2192            elem.click()  # Reopen the dropdown now that some objects exist.\n   452\u2192        result_container = self.selenium.find_element(\n   453\u2192            By.CSS_SELECTOR, \".select2-results\"\n   454\u2192        )\n   455\u2192        self.assertTrue(result_container.is_displayed())\n   456\u2192        # PAGINATOR_SIZE results and \"Loading more results\".\n   457\u2192        self.assertCountSeleniumElements(\n   458\u2192            \".select2-results__option\",\n   459\u2192            PAGINATOR_SIZE + 1,\n   460\u2192            root_element=result_container,\n   461\u2192        )\n   462\u2192        search = self.selenium.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, \".select2-search__field\")\n   463\u2192        # Load next page of results by scrolling to the bottom of the list.\n   464\u2192        for _ in range(PAGINATOR_SIZE + 1):\n   465\u2192            with self.select2_ajax_wait():\n   466\u2192                search.send_keys(Keys.ARROW_DOWN)\n   467\u2192        # All objects are now loaded.\n   468\u2192        self.assertCountSeleniumElements(\n   469\u2192            \".select2-results__option\",\n   470\u2192            PAGINATOR_SIZE + 11,\n   471\u2192            root_element=result_container,\n   472\u2192        )\n   473\u2192        # Limit the results with the search field.\n   474\u2192        with self.select2_ajax_wait():\n   475\u2192            search.send_keys(\"Who\")\n   476\u2192            # Ajax request is delayed.\n   477\u2192            self.assertTrue(result_container.is_displayed())\n   478\u2192            self.assertCountSeleniumElements(\n   479\u2192                \".select2-results__option\",\n   480\u2192                PAGINATOR_SIZE + 12,\n   481\u2192                root_element=result_container,\n   482\u2192            )\n   483\u2192        self.assertTrue(result_container.is_displayed())\n   484\u2192        self.assertCountSeleniumElements(\n   485\u2192            \".select2-results__option\", 1, root_element=result_container\n   486\u2192        )\n   487\u2192        # Select the result.\n   488\u2192        with self.select2_ajax_wait():\n   489\u2192            search.send_keys(Keys.RETURN)\n   490\u2192        select = Select(self.selenium.find_element(By.ID, \"id_question\"))\n   491\u2192        self.assertEqual(\n   492\u2192            select.first_selected_option.get_attribute(\"value\"), str(q1.pk)\n   493\u2192        )\n   494\u2192\n   495\u2192    def test_select_multiple(self):\n   496\u2192        from selenium.common import NoSuchElementException\n   497\u2192        from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By\n   498\u2192        from selenium.webdriver.common.keys import Keys\n   499\u2192        from selenium.webdriver.support.ui import Select\n   500\u2192\n   501\u2192        self.selenium.get(\n   502\u2192            self.live_server_url\n   503\u2192            + reverse(\"autocomplete_admin:admin_views_question_add\")\n   504\u2192        )\n   505\u2192        elem = self.selenium.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, \".select2-selection\")\n   506\u2192        with self.select2_ajax_wait():\n   507\u2192            elem.click()  # Open the autocomplete dropdown.\n   508\u2192        results = self.selenium.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, \".select2-results\")\n   509\u2192        self.assertTrue(results.is_displayed())\n   510\u2192        option = self.selenium.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, \".select2-results__option\")\n   511\u2192        self.assertEqual(option.text, \"No results found\")\n   512\u2192        with self.select2_ajax_wait():\n   513\u2192            elem.click()  # Close the autocomplete dropdown.\n   514\u2192 test_inline_add_another_widgets(self):\n   575\u2192        from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By\n   576\u2192\n   577\u2192        def assertNoResults(row):\n   578\u2192            elem = DateHierarchyTests(TestCase):\n   157\u2192    factory = RequestFactory()\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192    @classmethod\n   160\u2192    def setUpTestData(cls):\n   161\u2192        cls.superuser = User.objects.create_superuser(\n   162\u2192            username=\"super\", password=\"secret\", email=\"super@example.com\"\n   163\u2192        )\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192    def test_choice_links(self):\n   166\u2192        modeladmin = ModelAdmin(Question, site)\n   167\u2192        modeladmin.date_hierarchy = \"posted\"\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192        posted_dates = (\n   170\u2192            datetime.date(2017, 10, 1),\n   171\u2192            datetime.date(2017, 10, 1),\n   172\u2192            datetime.date(2017, 12, 15),\n   173\u2192 Question.objects.bulk_create(\n   178\u2192            Question(question=\"q\", posted=posted) for posted in posted_dates\n   179\u2192        )\n   180\u2192\n   181\u2192        tests = (\n   182\u2192            ({}, [[\"year=2017\"], [\"year=2018\"]]),\n   183\u2192            ({\"year\": 2016}, []),\n   184\u2192            ({\"year\": 2017}, [[\"month=10\", \"year=2017\"], [\"month=12\", \"year=2017\"]]),\n   185\u2192            ({\"year\": 2017, \"month\": 9}, []),\n   186\u2192            (\n   187\u2192                {\"year\": 2017, \"month\": 12},\n   188\u2192   192\u2192            ),\n   193\u2192        )\n   194\u2192        for query, expected_choices in tests:\n   195\u2192            with self.subTest(query=query):\n   196\u2192                query in spec[\"choices\"]]\n   202\u2192                expected_choices = [\n   203\u2192                    \"&\".join(\"posted__%s\" % c for c in choice)\n   204\u2192                    for choice in expected_choices\n   205\u2192                ]\n   206\u2192                expected_choices = [\n   207\u2192                    (\"?\" + choice) if choice else \"\" for choice in expected_choices\n   208\u2192                ]\n   209\u2192                self.assertEqual(choices, expected_choices)\n   210\u2192\n   211\u2192    def test_choice_links_datetime(self):\n   212\u2192 sync_waiter, test_filename\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192TEST_STATIC_ROOT = Path(__file__).parent / \"project\" / \"static\"\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192class SignalHandler:\n    37\u2192    \"\"\"Helper class to track threads and kwargs when signals are dispatched.\"\"\"\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192    def __init__(self):\n    40\u2192        super().__init__()\n    41\u2192        self.calls = []\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192    def __call__(self, signal, **kwargs):\n    44\u2192        self.calls.append({\"thread\": threading.current_thread(), \"kwargs\": kwargs})\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192@override_settings(ROOT_URLCONF=\"asgi.urls\")\n    48\u2192class ASGITest(SimpleTestCase):\n    49\u2192    async_request_factory = AsyncRequestFactory()\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192    def setUp(self):\n    52\u2192        request_started.disconnect(close_old_connections)\n    53\u2192        self.addCleanup(request_started.connect, close_old_connections)\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192    async def test_get_asgi_application(self):\n    56\u2192        \"\"\"\n    57\u2192        get_asgi_application() returns a functioning ASGI callable.\n    58\u2192        \"\"\"\n    59\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n    60\u2192        # Construct HTTP request.\n    61\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\")\n    62\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n    63\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n    64\u2192        # Read the response.\n    65\u2192        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n    66\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n    67\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n    68\u2192        self.assertEqual(\n    69\u2192            set(response_start[\"headers\"]),\n    70\u2192            {\n    71\u2192                (b\"Content-Length\", b\"12\"),\n    72\u2192                (b\"Content-Type\", b\"text/html; charset=utf-8\"),\n    73\u2192            },\n    74\u2192        )\n    75\u2192        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n    76\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n    77\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"Hello World!\")\n    78\u2192        # Allow response.close() to finish.\n    79\u2192        await communicator.wait()\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192    async def test_asgi_cookies(self):\n    82\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n    83\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/cookie/\")\n    84\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n    85\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n    86\u2192        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n    87\u2192        self.assertIn((b\"Set-Cookie\", b\"key=value; Path=/\"), response_start[\"headers\"])\n    88\u2192        # Allow response.close() to finish.\n    89\u2192        await communicator.wait()\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192    # Python's file API is not async compatible. A third-party library such\n    92\u2192    # as https://github.com/Tinche/aiofiles allows passing the file to\n    93\u2192    # FileResponse as an async iterator. With a sync iterator\n    94\u2192    # StreamingHTTPResponse triggers a warning when iterating the file.\n    95\u2192    # assertWarnsMessage is not async compatible, so ignore_warnings for the\n    96\u2192    # test.\n    97\u2192    @ignore_warnings(module=\"django.core.handlers.asgi\")\n    98\u2192    async def test_file_response(self):\n    99\u2192        \"\"\"\n   100\u2192        Makes sure that FileResponse works over ASGI.\n   101\u2192        \"\"\"\n   102\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   103\u2192        # Construct HTTP request.\n   104\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/file/\")\n   105\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   106\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   107\u2192        # Get the file content.\n   108\u2192        with open(test_filename, \"rb\") as test_file:\n   109\u2192            test_file_contents = test_file.read()\n   110\u2192        # Read the response.\n   111\u2192        with captured_stderr():\n   112\u2192            response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n   113\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n   114\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n   115\u2192        headers = response_start[\"headers\"]\n   116\u2192        self.assertEqual(len(headers), 3)\n   117\u2192        expected_headers = {\n   118\u2192            b\"Content-Length\": str(len(test_file_contents)).encode(\"ascii\"),\n   119\u2192            b\"Content-Type\": b\"text/x-python\",\n   120\u2192            b\"Content-Disposition\": b'inline; filename=\"urls.py\"',\n   121\u2192        }\n   122\u2192        for key, value in headers:\n   123\u2192            try:\n   124\u2192                self.assertEqual(value, expected_headers[key])\n   125\u2192            except AssertionError:\n   126\u2192                # Windows registry may not be configured with correct\n   127\u2192                # mimetypes.\n   128\u2192                if sys.platform == \"win32\" and key == b\"Content-Type\":\n   129\u2192                    self.assertEqual(value, b\"text/plain\")\n   130\u2192                else:\n   131\u2192                    raise\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192        # Warning ignored here.\n   134\u2192        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n   135\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n   136\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], test_file_contents)\n   137\u2192        # Allow response.close() to finish.\n   138\u2192        await communicator.wait()\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192    @modify_settings(INSTALLED_APPS={\"append\": \"django.contrib.staticfiles\"})\n   141\u2192    @override_settings(\n   142\u2192        STATIC_URL=\"static/\",\n   143\u2192        STATIC_ROOT=TEST_STATIC_ROOT,\n   144\u2192        STATICFILES_DIRS=[TEST_STATIC_ROOT],\n   145\u2192        STATICFILES_FINDERS=[\n   146\u2192            \"django.contrib.staticfiles.finders.FileSystemFinder\",\n   147\u2192        ],\n   148\u2192    )\n   149\u2192    async def test_static_file_response(self):\n   150\u2192        application = ASGIStaticFilesHandler(get_asgi_application())\n   151\u2192        # Construct HTTP request.\n   152\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/static/file.txt\")\n   153\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   154\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   155\u2192        # Get the file content.\n   156\u2192        file_path = TEST_STATIC_ROOT / \"file.txt\"\n   157\u2192        with open(file_path, \"rb\") as test_file:\n   158\u2192            test_file_contents = test_file.read()\n   159\u2192        # Read the response.\n   160\u2192        stat = file_path.stat()\n   161\u2192        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n   162\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n   163\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n   164\u2192        self.assertEqual(\n   165\u2192            set(response_start[\"headers\"]),\n   166\u2192            {\n   167\u2192                (b\"Content-Length\", str(len(test_file_contents)).encode(\"ascii\")),\n   168\u2192                (b\"Content-Type\", b\"text/plain\"),\n   169\u2192                (b\"Content-Disposition\", b'inline; filename=\"file.txt\"'),\n   170\u2192                (b\"Last-Modified\", http_date(stat.st_mtime).encode(\"ascii\")),\n   171\u2192            },\n   172\u2192        )\n   173\u2192        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n   174\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n   175\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], test_file_contents)\n   176\u2192        # Allow response.close() to finish.\n   177\u2192        await communicator.wait()\n   178\u2192\n   179\u2192    async def test_headers(self):\n   180\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   181\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(\n   182\u2192            application,\n   183\u2192            self.async_request_factory._base_scope(\n   184\u2192                path=\"/meta/\",\n   185\u2192                headers=[\n   186\u2192                    [b\"content-type\", b\"text/plain; charset=utf-8\"],\n   187\u2192                    [b\"content-length\", b\"77\"],\n   188\u2192                    [b\"referer\", b\"Scotland\"],\n   189\u2192                    [b\"referer\", b\"Wales\"],\n   190\u2192                ],\n   191\u2192            ),\n   192\u2192        )\n   193\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   194\u2192        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n   195\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n   196\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n   197\u2192        self.assertEqual(\n   198\u2192            set(response_start[\"headers\"]),\n   199\u2192            {\n   200\u2192                (b\"Content-Length\", b\"19\"),\n   201\u2192                (b\"Content-Type\", b\"text/plain; charset=utf-8\"),\n   202\u2192            },\n   203\u2192        )\n   204\u2192        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n   205\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n   206\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"From Scotland,Wales\")\n   207\u2192        # Allow response.close() to finish\n   208\u2192        await communicator.wait()\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192    async def test_post_body(self):\n   211\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   212\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(\n   213\u2192            method=\"POST\",\n   214\u2192            path=\"/post/\",\n   215\u2192            query_string=\"echo=1\",\n   216\u2192        )\n   217\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   218\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"Echo!\"})\n   219\u2192        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n   220\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n   221\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n   222\u2192        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n   223\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n   224\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"Echo!\")\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192    async def test_create_request_error(self):\n   227\u2192        # Track request_finished signal.\n   228\u2192        signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n   229\u2192        request_finished.connect(signal_handler)\n   230\u2192        self.addCleanup(request_finished.disconnect, signal_handler)\n   231\u2192\n   232\u2192        # Request class that always fails creation with RequestDataTooBig.\n   233\u2192        class TestASGIRequest(ASGIRequest):\n   234\u2192\n   235\u2192            def __init__(self, scope, body_file):\n   236\u2192                super().__init__(scope, body_file)\n   237\u2192                raise RequestDataTooBig()\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192        # Handler to use the custom request class.\n   240\u2192        class TestASGIHandler(ASGIHandler):\n   241\u2192            request_class = TestASGIRequest\n   242\u2192\n   243\u2192        application = TestASGIHandler()\n   244\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/not-important/\")\n   245\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   246\u2192\n   247\u2192        # Initiate request.\n   248\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   249\u2192        # Give response.close() time to finish.\n   250\u2192        await communicator.wait()\n   251\u2192\n   252\u2192        self.assertEqual(len(signal_handler.calls), 1)\n   253\u2192        self.assertNotEqual(\n   254\u2192            signal_handler.calls[0][\"thread\"], threading.current_thread()\n   255\u2192        )\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192    async def test_cancel_post_request_with_sync_processing(self):\n   258\u2192        \"\"\"\n   259\u2192        The request.body object should be available and readable in view\n   260\u2192        code, even if the ASGIHandler cancels processing part way through.\n   261\u2192        \"\"\"\n   262\u2192        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()\n   263\u2192        # Events to monitor the view processing from the parent test code.\n   264\u2192        view_started_event = asyncio.Event()\n   265\u2192        view_finished_event = asyncio.Event()\n   266\u2192        # Record received request body or exceptions raised in the test view\n   267\u2192        outcome = []\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192        # This view will run in a new thread because it is wrapped in\n   270\u2192        # sync_to_async. The view consumes the POST body data after a short\n   271\u2192        # delay. The test will cancel the request using http.disconnect during\n   272\u2192        # the delay, but because this is a sync view the code runs to\n   273\u2192        # completion. There should be no exceptions raised inside the view\n   274\u2192        # code.\n   275\u2192        @csrf_exempt\n   276\u2192        @sync_to_async\n   277\u2192        def post_view(request):\n   278\u2192            try:\n   279\u2192                loop.call_soon_threadsafe(view_started_event.set)\n   280\u2192                time.sleep(0.1)\n   281\u2192                # Do something to read request.body after pause\n   282\u2192                outcome.append({\"request_body\": request.body})\n   283\u2192                return HttpResponse(\"ok\")\n   284\u2192            except Exception as e:\n   285\u2192                outcome.append({\"exception\": e})\n   286\u2192            finally:\n   287\u2192                loop.call_soon_threadsafe(view_finished_event.set)\n   288\u2192\n   289\u2192        # Request class to use the view.\n   290\u2192        class TestASGIRequest(ASGIRequest):\n   291\u2192            urlconf = (path(\"post/\", post_view),)\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192        # Handler to use request class.\n   294\u2192        class TestASGIHandler(ASGIHandler):\n   295\u2192            request_class = TestASGIRequest\n   296\u2192\n   297\u2192        application = TestASGIHandler()\n   298\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(\n   299\u2192            method=\"POST\",\n   300\u2192            path=\"/post/\",\n   301\u2192        )\n   302\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   303\u2192\n   304\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"Body data!\"})\n   305\u2192\n   306\u2192        # Wait until the view code has started, then send http.disconnect.\n   307\u2192        await view_started_event.wait()\n   308\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n   309\u2192        # Wait until view code has finished.\n   310\u2192        await view_finished_event.wait()\n   311\u2192        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n   312\u2192            await communicator.receive_output()\n   313\u2192\n   314\u2192        self.assertEqual(outcome, [{\"request_body\": b\"Body data!\"}])\n   315\u2192\n   316\u2192    async def test_untouched_request_body_gets_closed(self):\n   317\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   318\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(method=\"POST\", path=\"/post/\")\n   319\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   320\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   321\u2192        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n   322\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n   323\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 204)\n   324\u2192        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n   325\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n   326\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"\")\n   327\u2192        # Allow response.close() to finish\n   328\u2192        await communicator.wait()\n   329\u2192\n   330\u2192    async def test_get_query_string(self):\n   331\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   332\u2192        for query_string in (b\"name=Andrew\", \"name=Andrew\"):\n   333\u2192            with self.subTest(query_string=query_string):\n   334\u2192                scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(\n   335\u2192                    path=\"/\",\n   336\u2192                    query_string=query_string,\n   337\u2192                )\n   338\u2192                communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   339\u2192                await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   340\u2192                response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n   341\u2192                self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n   342\u2192                self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n   343\u2192                response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n   344\u2192                self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n   345\u2192                self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"Hello Andrew!\")\n   346\u2192                # Allow response.close() to finish\n   347\u2192                await communicator.wait()\n   348\u2192\n   349\u2192    async def test_disconnect(self):\n   350\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   351\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\")\n   352\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   353\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n   354\u2192        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n   355\u2192            await communicator.receive_output()\n   356\u2192\n   357\u2192    async def test_disconnect_both_return(self):\n   358\u2192        # Force both the disconnect listener and the task that sends the\n   359\u2192        # response to finish at the same time.\n   360\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   361\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\")\n   362\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   363\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"some body\"})\n   364\u2192        # Fetch response headers (this yields to asyncio and causes\n   365\u2192        # ASGHandler.send_response() to dump the body of the response in the\n   366\u2192        # queue).\n   367\u2192        await communicator.receive_output()\n   368\u2192        # Fetch response body (there's already some data queued up, so this\n   369\u2192        # doesn't actually yield to the event loop, it just succeeds\n   370\u2192        # instantly).\n   371\u2192        await communicator.receive_output()\n   372\u2192        # Send disconnect at the same time that response finishes (this just\n   373\u2192        # puts some info in a queue, it doesn't have to yield to the event\n   374\u2192        # loop).\n   375\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n   376\u2192        # Waiting for the communicator _does_ yield to the event loop, since\n   377\u2192        # ASGIHandler.send_response() is still waiting to do response.close().\n   378\u2192        # It so happens that there are enough remaining yield points in both\n   379\u2192        # tasks that they both finish while the loop is running.\n   380\u2192        await communicator.wait()\n   381\u2192\n   382\u2192    async def test_disconnect_with_body(self):\n   383\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   384\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\")\n   385\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   386\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"some body\"})\n   387\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n   388\u2192        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n   389\u2192            await communicator.receive_output()\n   390\u2192\n   391\u2192    async def test_assert_in_listen_for_disconnect(self):\n   392\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   393\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\")\n   394\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   395\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   396\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.not_a_real_message\"})\n   397\u2192        msg = \"Invalid ASGI message after request body: http.not_a_real_message\"\n   398\u2192        with self.assertRaisesMessage(AssertionError, msg):\n   399\u2192            await communicator.wait()\n   400\u2192\n   401\u2192    async def test_delayed_disconnect_with_body(self):\n   402\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   403\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/delayed_hello/\")\n   404\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   405\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"some body\"})\n   406\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n   407\u2192        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n   408\u2192            await communicator.receive_output()\n   409\u2192\n   410\u2192    async def test_wrong_connection_type(self):\n   411\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   412\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\", type=\"other\")\n   413\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   414\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   415\u2192        msg = \"Django can only handle ASGI/HTTP connections, not other.\"\n   416\u2192        with self.assertRaisesMessage(ValueError, msg):\n   417\u2192            await communicator.receive_output()\n   418\u2192\n   419\u2192    async def test_non_unicode_query_string(self):\n   420\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   421\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\", query_string=b\"\\xff\")\n   422\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   423\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   424\u2192        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n   425\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n   426\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 400)\n   427\u2192        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n   428\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n   429\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"\")\n   430\u2192\n   431\u2192    async def test_request_lifecycle_signals_dispatched_with_thread_sensitive(self):\n   432\u2192        # Track request_started and request_finished signals.\n   433\u2192        signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n   434\u2192        request_started.connect(signal_handler)\n   435\u2192        self.addCleanup(request_started.disconnect, signal_handler)\n   436\u2192        request_finished.connect(signal_handler)\n   437\u2192        self.addCleanup(request_finished.disconnect, signal_handler)\n   438\u2192\n   439\u2192        # Perform a basic request.\n   440\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   441\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\")\n   442\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   443\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   444\u2192        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n   445\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n   446\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n   447\u2192        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n   448\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n   449\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"Hello World!\")\n   450\u2192        # Give response.close() time to finish.\n   451\u2192        await communicator.wait()\n   452\u2192\n   453\u2192        # AsyncToSync should have executed the signals in the same thread.\n   454\u2192        self.assertEqual(len(signal_handler.calls), 2)\n   455\u2192        request_started_call, request_finished_call = signal_handler.calls\n   456\u2192        self.assertEqual(\n   457\u2192            request_started_call[\"thread\"], request_finished_call[\"thread\"]\n   458\u2192        )\n   459\u2192\n   460\u2192    async def test_concurrent_async_uses_multiple_thread_pools(self):\n   461\u2192        sync_waiter.active_threads.clear()\n   462\u2192\n   463\u2192        # Send 2 requests concurrently\n   464\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   465\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/wait/\")\n   466\u2192        communicators = []\n   467\u2192        for _ in range(2):\n   468\u2192            communicators.append(ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope))\n   469\u2192            await communicators[-1].send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   470\u2192\n   471\u2192        # Each request must complete with a status code of 200\n   472\u2192        # If requests aren't scheduled concurrently, the barrier in the\n   473\u2192        # sync_wait view will time out, resulting in a 500 status code.\n   474\u2192        for communicator in communicators:\n   475\u2192            response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n   476\u2192            self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n   477\u2192            self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n   478\u2192            response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n   479\u2192            self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n   480\u2192            self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"Hello World!\")\n   481\u2192            # Give response.close() time to finish.\n   482\u2192            await communicator.wait()\n   483\u2192\n   484\u2192        # The requests should have scheduled on different threads. Note\n   485\u2192        # active_threads is a set (a thread can only appear once), therefore\n   486\u2192        # length is a sufficient check.\n   487\u2192        self.assertEqual(len(sync_waiter.active_threads), 2)\n   488\u2192\n   489\u2192        sync_waiter.active_threads.clear()\n   490\u2192\n   491\u2192    async def test_asyncio_cancel_error(self):\n   492\u2192        view_started = asyncio.Event()\n   493\u2192        # Flag to check if the view was cancelled.\n   494\u2192        view_did_cancel = False\n   495\u2192        # Track request_finished signal.\n   496\u2192        signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n   497\u2192        request_finished.connect(signal_handler)\n   498\u2192        self.addCleanup(request_finished.disconnect, signal_handler)\n   499\u2192\n   500\u2192        # A view that will listen for the cancelled error.\n   501\u2192        async def view(request):\n   502\u2192            nonlocal view_did_cancel\n   503\u2192            view_started.set()\n   504\u2192            try:\n   505\u2192                await asyncio.sleep(0.1)\n   506\u2192                return HttpResponse(\"Hello World!\")\n   507\u2192            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n   508\u2192                # Set the flag.\n   509\u2192                view_did_cancel = True\n   510\u2192                raise\n   511\u2192\n   512\u2192        # Request class to use the view.\n   513\u2192        class TestASGIRequest(ASGIRequest):\n   514\u2192            urlconf = (path(\"cancel/\", view),)\n   515\u2192\n   516\u2192        # Handler to use request class.\n   517\u2192        class TestASGIHandler(ASGIHandler):\n   518\u2192            request_class = TestASGIRequest\n   519\u2192\n   520\u2192        # Request cycle should complete since no disconnect was sent.\n   521\u2192        application = TestASGIHandler()\n   522\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/cancel/\")\n   523\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   524\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   525\u2192        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n   526\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n   527\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n   528\u2192        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n   529\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n   530\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"Hello World!\")\n   531\u2192        # Give response.close() time to finish.\n   532\u2192        await communicator.wait()\n   533\u2192        self.assertIs(view_did_cancel, False)\n   534\u2192        # Exactly one call to request_finished handler.\n   535\u2192        self.assertEqual(len(signal_handler.calls), 1)\n   536\u2192        handler_call = signal_handler.calls.pop()\n   537\u2192        # It was NOT on the async thread.\n   538\u2192        self.assertNotEqual(handler_call[\"thread\"], threading.current_thread())\n   539\u2192        # The signal sender is the handler class.\n   540\u2192        self.assertEqual(handler_call[\"kwargs\"], {\"sender\": TestASGIHandler})\n   541\u2192        view_started.clear()\n   542\u2192\n   543\u2192        # Request cycle with a disconnect before the view can respond.\n   544\u2192        application = TestASGIHandler()\n   545\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/cancel/\")\n   546\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   547\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   548\u2192        # Let the view actually start.\n   549\u2192        await view_started.wait()\n   550\u2192        # Disconnect the client.\n   551\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n   552\u2192        # The handler should not send a response.\n   553\u2192        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n   554\u2192            await communicator.receive_output()\n   555\u2192        await communicator.wait()\n   556\u2192        self.assertIs(view_did_cancel, True)\n   557\u2192        # Exactly one call to request_finished handler.\n   558\u2192        self.assertEqual(len(signal_handler.calls), 1)\n   559\u2192        handler_call = signal_handler.calls.pop()\n   560\u2192        # It was NOT on the async thread.\n   561\u2192        self.assertNotEqual(handler_call[\"thread\"], threading.current_thread())\n   562\u2192        # The signal sender is the handler class.\n   563\u2192        self.assertEqual(handler_call[\"kwargs\"], {\"sender\": TestASGIHandler})\n   564\u2192\n   565\u2192    async def test_asyncio_streaming_cancel_error(self):\n   566\u2192        # Similar to test_asyncio_cancel_error(), but during a streaming\n   567\u2192        # response.\n   568\u2192        view_did_cancel = False\n   569\u2192        # Track request_finished signals.\n   570\u2192        signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n   571\u2192        request_finished.connect(signal_handler)\n   572\u2192        self.addCleanup(request_finished.disconnect, signal_handler)\n   573\u2192\n   574\u2192        async def streaming_response():\n   575\u2192            nonlocal view_did_cancel\n   576\u2192            try:\n   577\u2192                await asyncio.sleep(0.2)\n   578\u2192                yield b\"Hello World!\"\n   579\u2192            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n   580\u2192                # Set the flag.\n   581\u2192                view_did_cancel = True\n   582\u2192                raise\n   583\u2192\n   584\u2192        async def view(request):\n   585\u2192            return StreamingHttpResponse(streaming_response())\n   586\u2192\n   587\u2192        class TestASGIRequest(ASGIRequest):\n   588\u2192            urlconf = (path(\"cancel/\", view),)\n   589\u2192\n   590\u2192        class TestASGIHandler(ASGIHandler):\n   591\u2192            request_class = TestASGIRequest\n   592\u2192\n   593\u2192        # With no disconnect, the request cycle should complete in the same\n   594\u2192        # manner as the non-streaming response.\n   595\u2192        application = TestASGIHandler()\n   596\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/cancel/\")\n   597\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   598\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   599\u2192        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n   600\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n   601\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n   602\u2192        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n   603\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n   604\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"Hello World!\")\n   605\u2192        await communicator.wait()\n   606\u2192        self.assertIs(view_did_cancel, False)\n   607\u2192        # Exactly one call to request_finished handler.\n   608\u2192        self.assertEqual(len(signal_handler.calls), 1)\n   609\u2192        handler_call = signal_handler.calls.pop()\n   610\u2192        # It was NOT on the async thread.\n   611\u2192        self.assertNotEqual(handler_call[\"thread\"], threading.current_thread())\n   612\u2192        # The signal sender is the handler class.\n   613\u2192        self.assertEqual(handler_call[\"kwargs\"], {\"sender\": TestASGIHandler})\n   614\u2192\n   615\u2192        # Request cycle with a disconnect.\n   616\u2192        application = TestASGIHandler()\n   617\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/cancel/\")\n   618\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   619\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   620\u2192        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n   621\u2192        # Fetch the start of response so streaming can begin\n   622\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n   623\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n   624\u2192        await asyncio.sleep(0.1)\n   625\u2192        # Now disconnect the client.\n   626\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n   627\u2192        # This time the handler should not send a response.\n   628\u2192        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n   629\u2192            await communicator.receive_output()\n   630\u2192        await communicator.wait()\n   631\u2192        self.assertIs(view_did_cancel, True)\n   632\u2192        # Exactly one call to request_finished handler.\n   633\u2192        self.assertEqual(len(signal_handler.calls), 1)\n   634\u2192        handler_call = signal_handler.calls.pop()\n   635\u2192        # It was NOT on the async thread.\n   636\u2192        self.assertNotEqual(handler_call[\"thread\"], threading.current_thread())\n   637\u2192        # The signal sender is the handler class.\n   638\u2192        self.assertEqual(handler_call[\"kwargs\"], {\"sender\": TestASGIHandler})\n   639\u2192\n   640\u2192    async def test_streaming(self):\n   641\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(\n   642\u2192            path=\"/streaming/\", query_string=b\"sleep=0.001\"\n   643\u2192        )\n   644\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   645\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   646\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   647\u2192        # Fetch http.response.start.\n   648\u2192        await communicator.receive_output(timeout=1)\n   649\u2192        # Fetch the 'first' and 'last'.\n   650\u2192        first_response = await communicator.receive_output(timeout=1)\n   651\u2192        self.assertEqual(first_response[\"body\"], b\"first\\n\")\n   652\u2192        second_response = await communicator.receive_output(timeout=1)\n   653\u2192        self.assertEqual(second_response[\"body\"], b\"last\\n\")\n   654\u2192        # Fetch the rest of the response so that coroutines are cleaned up.\n   655\u2192        await communicator.receive_output(timeout=1)\n   656\u2192        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n   657\u2192            await communicator.receive_output(timeout=1)\n   658\u2192\n   659\u2192    async def test_streaming_disconnect(self):\n   660\u2192        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(\n   661\u2192            path=\"/streaming/\", query_string=b\"sleep=0.1\"\n   662\u2192        )\n   663\u2192        application = get_asgi_application()\n   664\u2192        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n   665\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n   666\u2192        await communicator.receive_output(timeout=1)\n   667\u2192        first_response = await communicator.receive_output(timeout=1)\n   668\u2192        self.assertEqual(first_response[\"body\"], b\"first\\n\")\n   669\u2192        # Disconnect the client.\n   670\u2192        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n   671\u2192        # 'last\\n' isn't sent.\n   672\u2192        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n   673\u2192            await communicator.receive_output(timeout=0.2)\n   674\u2192\n   675\u2192    async def test_read_body_thread(self):\n   676\u2192        \"\"\"Write runs on correct thread depending on rollover.\"\"\"\n   677\u2192        handler = ASGIHandler()\n   678\u2192        loop_thread = threading.current_thread()\n   679\u2192\n   680\u2192        called_threads = []\n   681\u2192\n   682\u2192        def write_wrapper(data):\n   683\u2192            called_threads.append(threading.current_thread())\n   684\u2192            return original_write(data)\n   685\u2192\n   686\u2192        # In-memory write (no rollover expected).\n   687\u2192        in_memory_chunks = [\n   688\u2192            {\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"small\", \"more_body\": False}\n   689\u2192        ]\n   690\u2192\n   691\u2192        async def receive():\n   692\u2192            return in_memory_chunks.pop(0)\n   693\u2192\n   694\u2192        with tempfile.SpooledTemporaryFile(max_size=1024, mode=\"w+b\") as temp_file:\n   695\u2192            original_write = temp_file.write\n   696\u2192            with (\n   697\u2192                patch(\n   698\u2192                    \"django.core.handlers.asgi.tempfile.SpooledTemporaryFile\",\n   699\u2192                    return_value=temp_file,\n   700\u2192                ),\n   701\u2192                patch.object(temp_file, \"write\", side_effect=write_wrapper),\n   702\u2192            ):\n   703\u2192                await handler.read_body(receive)\n   704\u2192        # Write was called in the event loop thread.\n   705\u2192        self.assertIn(loop_thread, called_threads)\n   706\u2192\n   707\u2192        # Clear thread log before next test.\n   708\u2192        called_threads.clear()\n   709\u2192\n   710\u2192        # Rollover to disk (write should occur in a threadpool thread).\n   711\u2192        rolled_chunks = [\n   712\u2192            {\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"A\" * 16, \"more_body\": True},\n   713\u2192            {\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"B\" * 16, \"more_body\": False},\n   714\u2192        ]\n   715\u2192\n   716\u2192        async def receive_rolled():\n   717\u2192            return rolled_chunks.pop(0)\n   718\u2192\n   719\u2192        with (\n   720\u2192            override_settings(FILE_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE=10),\n   721\u2192            tempfile.SpooledTemporaryFile(max_size=10, mode=\"w+b\") as temp_file,\n   722\u2192        ):\n   723\u2192            original_write = temp_file.write\n   724\u2192            # roll_over force in handlers.\n   725\u2192            with (\n   726\u2192                patch(\n   727\u2192                    \"django.core.handlers.asgi.tempfile.SpooledTemporaryFile\",\n   728\u2192                    return_value=temp_file,\n   729\u2192                ),\n   730\u2192                patch.object(temp_file, \"write\", side_effect=write_wrapper),\n   731\u2192            ):\n   732\u2192                await handler.read_body(receive_rolled)\n   733\u2192        # The second write should have rolled over to disk.\n   734\u2192        self.assertTrue(any(t != loop_thread for t in called_threads))\n   735\u2192\n   736\u2192    def test_multiple_cookie_headers_http2(self):\n   737\u2192        test_cases = [\n   738\u2192            {\n   739\u2192                \"label\": \"RFC-compliant headers (no semicolon)\",\n   740\u2192                \"headers\": [\n   741\u2192                    (b\"cookie\", b\"a=abc\"),\n   742\u2192                    (b\"cookie\", b\"b=def\"),\n   743\u2192                    (b\"cookie\", b\"c=ghi\"),\n   744\u2192                ],\n   745\u2192            },\n   746\u2192            {\n   747\u2192                # Some clients may send cookies with trailing semicolons.\n   748\u2192                \"label\": \"Headers with trailing semicolons\",\n   749\u2192                \"headers\": [\n   750\u2192                    (b\"cookie\", b\"a=abc;\"),\n   751\u2192                    (b\"cookie\", b\"b=def;\"),\n   752\u2192                    (b\"cookie\", b\"c=ghi;\"),\n   753\u2192                ],\n   754\u2192            },\n   755\u2192        ]\n   756\u2192\n   757\u2192        for case in test_cases:\n   758\u2192            with self.subTest(case[\"label\"]):\n   759\u2192                scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(\n   760\u2192                    path=\"/\", http_version=\"2.0\"\n   761\u2192                )\n   762\u2192                scope[\"headers\"] = case[\"headers\"]\n   763\u2192                request = ASGIRequest(scope, None)\n   764\u2192                self.assertEqual(request.META[\"HTTP_COOKIE\"], \"a=abc; b=def; c=ghi\")\n   765\u2192                self.assertEqual(request.COOKIES, {\"a\": \"abc\", \"b\": \"def\", \"c\": \"ghi\"})\n   766\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"     1\u2192import asyncio\n     2\u2192import os\n     3\u2192from inspect import iscoroutinefunction\n     4\u2192from unittest import mock\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192from asgiref.sync import async_to_sync\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192from django.core.cache import DEFAULT_CACHE_ALIAS, caches\n     9\u2192from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured, SynchronousOnlyOperation\n    10\u2192from django.http import HttpResponse, HttpResponseNotAllowed\n    11\u2192from django.test import RequestFactory, SimpleTestCase\n    12\u2192from django.utils.asyncio import async_unsafe\n    13\u2192from django.views.generic.base import View\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192from .models import SimpleModel\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192class CacheTest(SimpleTestCase):\n    19\u2192    def test_caches_local(self):\n    20\u2192        @async_to_sync\n    21\u2192        async def async_cache():\n    22\u2192            return caches[DEFAULT_CACHE_ALIAS]\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192        cache_1 = async_cache()\n    25\u2192        cache_2 = async_cache()\n    26\u2192        self.assertIs(cache_1, cache_2)\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192class DatabaseConnectionTest(SimpleTestCase):\n    30\u2192    \"\"\"A database connection cannot be used in an async context.\"\"\"\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192    async def test_get_async_connection(self):\n    33\u2192        with self.assertRaises(SynchronousOnlyOperation):\n    34\u2192            list(SimpleModel.objects.all())\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192class AsyncUnsafeTest(SimpleTestCase):\n    38\u2192    \"\"\"\n    39\u2192    async_unsafe decorator should work correctly and returns the correct\n    40\u2192    message.\n    41\u2192    \"\"\"\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192    @async_unsafe\n    44\u2192    def dangerous_method(self):\n    45\u2192        return True\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192    async def test_async_unsafe(self):\n    48\u2192        # async_unsafe decorator catches bad access and returns the right\n    49\u2192        # message.\n    50\u2192        msg = (\n    51\u2192            \"You cannot call this from an async context - use a thread or \"\n    52\u2192            \"sync_to_async.\"\n    53\u2192        )\n    54\u2192        with self.assertRaisesMessage(SynchronousOnlyOperation, msg):\n    55\u2192            self.dangerous_method()\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192    @mock.patch.dict(os.environ, {\"DJANGO_ALLOW_ASYNC_UNSAFE\": \"true\"})\n    58\u2192    @async_to_sync  # mock.patch() is not async-aware.\n    59\u2192    async def test_async_unsafe_suppressed(self):\n    60\u2192        # Decorator doesn't trigger check when the environment variable to\n    61\u2192        # suppress it is set.\n    62\u2192        try:\n    63\u2192            self.dangerous_method()\n    64\u2192        except SynchronousOnlyOperation:\n    65\u2192            self.fail(\"SynchronousOnlyOperation should not be raised.\")\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192class SyncView(View):\n    69\u2192    def get(self, request, *args, **kwargs):\n    70\u2192        return HttpResponse(\"Hello (sync) world!\")\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192class AsyncView(View):\n    74\u2192    async def get(self, request, *args, **kwargs):\n    75\u2192        return HttpResponse(\"Hello (async) world!\")\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192class ViewTests(SimpleTestCase):\n    79\u2192    def test_views_are_correctly_marked(self):\n    80\u2192        tests = [\n    81\u2192            (SyncView, False),\n    82\u2192            (AsyncView, True),\n    83\u2192        ]\n    84\u2192        for view_cls, is_async in tests:\n    85\u2192            with self.subTest(view_cls=view_cls, is_async=is_async):\n    86\u2192                self.assertIs(view_cls.view_is_async, is_async)\n    87\u2192                callback = view_cls.as_view()\n    88\u2192                self.assertIs(iscoroutinefunction(callback), is_async)\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192    def test_mixed_views_raise_error(self):\n    91\u2192        class MixedView(View):\n    92\u2192            def get(self, request, *args, **kwargs):\n    93\u2192                return HttpResponse(\"Hello (mixed) world!\")\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192            async def post(self, request, *args, **kwargs):\n    96\u2192                return HttpResponse(\"Hello (mixed) world!\")\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192        msg = (\n    99\u2192            f\"{MixedView.__qualname__} HTTP handlers must either be all sync or all \"\n   100\u2192            \"async.\"\n   101\u2192        )\n   102\u2192        with self.assertRaisesMessage(ImproperlyConfigured, msg):\n   103\u2192            MixedView.as_view()\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192    def test_options_handler_responds_correctly(self):\n   106\u2192        tests = [\n   107\u2192            (SyncView, False),\n   108\u2192            (AsyncView, True),\n   109\u2192        ]\n   110\u2192        for view_cls, is_coroutine in tests:\n   111\u2192            with self.subTest(view_cls=view_cls, is_coroutine=is_coroutine):\n   112\u2192                instance = view_cls()\n   113\u2192                response = instance.options(None)\n   114\u2192                self.assertIs(\n   115\u2192                    asyncio.iscoroutine(response),\n   116\u2192                    is_coroutine,\n   117\u2192                )\n   118\u2192                if is_coroutine:\n   119\u2192                    response = asyncio.run(response)\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192                self.assertIsInstance(response, HttpResponse)\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192    def test_http_method_not_allowed_responds_correctly(self):\n   124\u2192        request_factory = RequestFactory()\n   125\u2192        tests = [\n   126\u2192            (SyncView, False),\n   127\u2192            (AsyncView, True),\n   128\u2192        ]\n   129\u2192        for view_cls, is_coroutine in tests:\n   130\u2192            with self.subTest(view_cls=view_cls, is_coroutine=is_coroutine):\n   131\u2192                instance = view_cls()\n   132\u2192                response = instance.http_method_not_allowed(request_factory.post(\"/\"))\n   133\u2192                self.assertIs(\n   134\u2192                    asyncio.iscoroutine(response),\n   135\u2192                    is_coroutine,\n   136\u2192                )\n   137\u2192                if is_coroutine:\n   138\u2192                    response = asyncio.run(response)\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192                self.assertIsInstance(response, HttpResponseNotAllowed)\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192    def test_base_view_class_is_sync(self):\n   143\u2192        \"\"\"\n   144\u2192        View and by extension any subclasses that don't define handlers are\n   145\u2192        sync.\n   146\u2192        \"\"\"\n   147\u2192        self.assertIs(View.view_is_async, False)\n   148\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered
