"     1\u2192import sys\n     2\u2192import traceback\n     3\u2192from io import BytesIO\n     4\u2192from unittest import TestCase, mock\n     5\u2192from wsgiref import simple_server\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192from django.core.servers.basehttp import get_internal_wsgi_application\n     8\u2192from django.core.signals import request_finished\n     9\u2192from django.test import RequestFactory, override_settings\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192from .views import FILE_RESPONSE_HOLDER\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192# If data is too large, socket will choke, so write chunks no larger than 32MB\n    14\u2192# at a time. The rationale behind the 32MB can be found in #5596#comment:4.\n    15\u2192MAX_SOCKET_CHUNK_SIZE = 32 * 1024 * 1024  # 32 MB\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192class ServerHandler(simple_server.ServerHandler):\n    19\u2192    error_status = \"500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR\"\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192    def write(self, data):\n    22\u2192        \"\"\"'write()' callable as specified by PEP 3333\"\"\"\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192        assert isinstance(data, bytes), \"write() argument must be bytestring\"\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192        if not self.status:\n    27\u2192            raise AssertionError(\"write() before start_response()\")\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192        elif not self.headers_sent:\n    30\u2192            # Before the first output, send the stored headers\n    31\u2192            self.bytes_sent = len(data)  # make sure we know content-length\n    32\u2192            self.send_headers()\n    33\u2192        else:\n    34\u2192            self.bytes_sent += len(data)\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192        # XXX check Content-Length and truncate if too many bytes written?\n    37\u2192        data = BytesIO(data)\n    38\u2192        for chunk in iter(lambda: data.read(MAX_SOCKET_CHUNK_SIZE), b\"\"):\n    39\u2192            self._write(chunk)\n    40\u2192            self._flush()\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192    def error_output(self, environ, start_response):\n    43\u2192        super().error_output(environ, start_response)\n    44\u2192        return [\"\\n\".join(traceback.format_exception(*sys.exc_info()))]\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192class DummyHandler:\n    48\u2192    def log_request(self, *args, **kwargs):\n    49\u2192        pass\n    50\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

6\u2192\n     7\u2192class ApiTests(SimpleTestCase):\n     8\u2192    rf = RequestFactory()\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192    def setUp(self):\n    11\u2192        self.request = self.rf.request()\n    12\u2192        self.storage = DummyStorage()\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192    def test_ok(self):\n    15\u2192        msg = \"some message\"\n    16\u2192        self.request._messages = self.storage\n    17\u2192        messages.add_message(self.request, messages.DEBUG, msg)\n    18\u2192        [message] = self.storage.store\n    19\u2192        self.assertEqual(msg, message.message)\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192    def test_request_is_none(self):\n    22\u2192        msg = \"add_message() argument must be an HttpRequest object, not 'NoneType'.\"\n    23\u2192        self.request._messages = self.storage\n    24\u2192        with self.assertRaisesMessage(TypeError, msg):\n    25\u2192            messages.add_message(None, messages.DEBUG, \"some message\")\n    26\u2192        self.assertEqual(self.storage.store, [])\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192    def test_middleware_missing(self):\n    29\u2192        msg = (\n    30\u2192            \"You cannot add messages without installing \"\n    31\u2192            \"django.contrib.messages.middleware.MessageMiddleware\"\n    32\u2192        )\n    33\u2192        with self.assertRaisesMessage(messages.MessageFailure, msg):\n    34\u2192            messages.add_message(self.request, messages.DEBUG, \"some message\")\n    35\u2192        self.assertEqual(self.storage.store, [])\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192    def test_middleware_missing_silently(self):\n    38\u2192        messages.add_message(\n    39\u2192            self.request, messages.DEBUG, \"some message\", fail_silently=True\n    40\u2192        )\n    41\u2192        self.assertEqual(self.storage.store, [])\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192class 5\u2192\n     6\u2192class SecurityMiddlewareTest(SimpleTestCase):\n     7\u2192    def middleware(self, *args, **kwargs):\n     8\u2192        from django.middleware.security import SecurityMiddleware\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192        return SecurityMiddleware(self.response(*args, **kwargs))\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192    @property\n    13\u2192    def secure_request_kwargs(self):\n    14\u2192        return {\"wsgi.url_scheme\": \"https\"}\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192    def response(self, *args, headers=None, **kwargs):\n    17\u2192        def get_response(req):\n    18\u2192            response = HttpResponse(*args, **kwargs)\n    19\u2192            if headers:\n    20\u2192                for k, v in headers.items():\n    21\u2192                    response.headers[k] = v\n    22\u2192            return response\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192        return get_response\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192    def process_response(self, *args, secure=False, request=None, **kwargs):\n    27\u2192        request_kwargs = {}\n    28\u2192        if secure:\n    29\u2192            request_kwargs.update(self.secure_request_kwargs)\n    30\u2192        if request is None:\n    31\u2192            request = self.request.get(\"/some/url\", **request_kwargs)\n    32\u2192        ret = self.middleware(*args, **kwargs).process_request(request)\n    33\u2192        if ret:\n    34\u2192            return ret\n    35\u2192        return self.middleware(*args, **kwargs)(request)\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192    request = RequestFactory()\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192    def process_request(self, method, *args, secure=False, **kwargs):\n    40\u2192        if secure:\n    41\u2192            kwargs.update(self.secure_request_kwargs)\n    42\u2192        req = getattr(self.request, method.lower())(*args, **kwargs)\n    43\u2192        return self.middleware().process_request(req)\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192    @override_settings(SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS=3600)\n    46\u2192    def test_sts_on(self):\n    47\u2192        \"\"\"\n    48\u2192        With SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS=3600, the middleware adds\n    49\u2192        \"Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=3600\" to the response.\n    50\u2192        \"\"\"\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. get_response_404(request):\n    36\u2192    return HttpResponseNotFound()\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192@override_settings(ROOT_URLCONF=\"middleware.urls\")\n    40\u2192class test_append_slash_have_slash(self):\n    45\u2192        \"\"\"\n    46\u2192        URLs with slashes should go unmolested.\n    47\u2192        \"\"\"\n    48\u2192        request = self.rf.get(\"/slash/\")\n    49\u2192        self.assertIsNone(CommonMiddleware(get_response_404).process_request(request))\n    50\u2192        self.assertEqual(CommonMiddleware(get_response_404)(request).status_code, 404)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. AsyncRequestFactory\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192from .cases import StaticFilesTestCase\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192class MockApplication:\n     9\u2192    \"\"\"ASGI application that returns a string indicating that it was called.\"\"\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):\n    12\u2192        return \"Application called\"\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192class TestASGIStaticFilesHandler(StaticFilesTestCase):\n    16\u2192    async_request_factory = AsyncRequestFactory()\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192    async def test_get_async_response(self):\n    19\u2192        request = self.async_request_factory.get(\"/static/test/file.txt\")\n    20\u2192        handler = ASGIStaticFilesHandler(ASGIHandler())\n    21\u2192        response = await handler.get_response_async(request)\n    22\u2192        response.close()\n    23\u2192        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 200)\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192    async def test_get_async_response_not_found(self):\n    26\u2192        request = self.async_request_factory.get(\"/static/test/not-found.txt\")\n    27\u2192        handler = ASGIStaticFilesHandler(ASGIHandler())\n    28\u2192        response = await handler.get_response_async(request)\n    29\u2192        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 404)\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192    async def test_non_http_requests_passed_to_the_wrapped_application(self):\n    32\u2192        tests = [\n    33\u2192            \"/static/path.txt\",\n    34\u2192            \"/non-static/path.txt\",\n    35\u2192        ]\n    36\u2192        for path in tests:\n    37\u2192            with self.subTest(path=path):\n    38\u2192                scope = {\"type\": \"websocket\", \"path\": path}\n    39\u2192                handler = ASGIStaticFilesHandler(MockApplication())\n    40\u2192                response = await handler(scope, None, None)\n    41\u2192 See ticket #23789.\n    20\u2192        engine = DjangoTemplates(\n    21\u2192            {\n    22\u2192                \"DIRS\": [],\n    23\u2192                \"APP_DIRS\": False,\n    24\u2192                \"NAME\": \"django\",\n    25\u2192                \"OPTIONS\": {\n    26\u2192                    \"context_processors\": [test_processor_name],\n    27\u2192                },\n    28\u2192            }\n    29\u2192        )\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192        template = engine.from_string(\"{{ processors }}\")\n    32\u2192        request = self.request_factory.get(\"/\")\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192        # Context processors run\n    35\u2192        content = template.render({}, request)\n    36\u2192        self.assertEqual(content, \"yes\")\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192        # Context overrides context processors\n    39\u2192        content = template.render({\"processors\": \"no\"}, request)\n    40\u2192        self.assertEqual(content, \"no\")\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192    def test_render_requires_dict(self):\n    43\u2192        \"\"\"django.Template.render() requires a dict.\"\"\"\n    44\u2192        engine = DjangoTemplates(\n    45\u2192            {\n    46\u2192                \"DIRS\": [],\n    47\u2192                \"APP_DIRS\": False,\n    48\u2192                \"NAME\": \"django\",\n    49\u2192                \"OPTIONS\": {},\n    50\u2192            }\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. "     1\u2192from pathlib import Path\n     2\u2192from unittest import mock, skipIf\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192from django.contrib.auth.models import User\n     5\u2192from django.template import TemplateSyntaxError\n     6\u2192from django.test import RequestFactory, TestCase\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192from .test_dummy import TemplateStringsTests\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192try:\n    11\u2192    import jinja2\n    12\u2192except ImportError:\n    13\u2192    jinja2 = None\n    14\u2192    Jinja2 = None\n    15\u2192else:\n    16\u2192    from django.template.backends.jinja2 import Jinja2\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192@skipIf(jinja2 is None, \"this test requires jinja2\")\n    20\u2192class Jinja2Tests(TemplateStringsTests):\n    21\u2192    engine_class = Jinja2\n    22\u2192    backend_name = \"jinja2\"\n    23\u2192    options = {\n    24\u2192        \"keep_trailing_newline\": True,\n    25\u2192        \"context_processors\": [\n    26\u2192            \"django.template.context_processors.static\",\n    27\u2192        ],\n    28\u2192    }\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192    def test_origin(self):\n    31\u2192        template = self.engine.get_template(\"template_backends/hello.html\")\n    32\u2192        self.assertTrue(template.origin.name.endswith(\"hello.html\"))\n    33\u2192        self.assertEqual(template.origin.template_name, \"template_backends/hello.html\")\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192    def test_origin_from_string(self):\n    36\u2192        template = self.engine.from_string(\"Hello!\\n\")\n    37\u2192        self.assertEqual(template.origin.name, \"<template>\")\n    38\u2192        self.assertIsNone(template.origin.template_name)\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192    def test_self_context(self):\n    41\u2192        \"\"\"\n    42\u2192        Using 'self' in the context should not throw errors (#24538).\n    43\u2192        \"\"\"\n    44\u2192        # self will be overridden to be a TemplateReference, so the self\n    45\u2192        # variable will not come through. Attempting to use one though should\n    46\u2192        # not throw an error.\n    47\u2192        template = self.engine.from_string(\"hello {{ foo }}!\")\n    48\u2192        content = template.render(context={\"self\": \"self\", \"foo\": \"world\"})\n    49\u2192        self.assertEqual(content, \"hello world!\")\n    50\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

import RequestFactory, SimpleTestCase\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192from ..utils import setup\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192class QueryStringTagTests(SimpleTestCase):\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192    request_factory = RequestFactory()\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192    def assertRenderEqual(self, template_name, context, expected):\n    14\u2192        output = self.engine.render_to_string(template_name, context)\n    15\u2192        self.assertEqual(output, expected)\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192    def assertTemplateSyntaxError(self, template_name, context, expected):\n    18\u2192        with self.assertRaisesMessage(TemplateSyntaxError, expected):\n    19\u2192            self.engine.render_to_string(template_name, context)\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192 RequestContext(self.request_factory.get(\"/\"))\n    24\u2192        self.assertRenderEqual(\"querystring_empty_get_params\", context, expected=\"?\")\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192    @setup({\"querystring_remove_all_params\": \"{% querystring a=None %}\"})\n    27\u2192    def test_querystring_remove_all_params(self):\n    28\u2192        non_empty_context = RequestContext(self.request_factory.get(\"/?a=b\"))\n    29\u2192        empty_context = RequestContext(self.request_factory.get(\"/\"))\n    30\u2192        for context in [non_empty_context, empty_context]:\n    31\u2192            with self.subTest(context=context):\n    32\u2192                self.assertRenderEqual(\"querystring_remove_all_params\", context, \"?\")\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192    @setup(\n    35\u2192        {\n    36\u2192            \"querystring_remove_all_params_custom_querydict\": (\n    37\u2192                \"{% querystring my_query_dict my_dict a=None %}\"\n    38\u2192            )\n    39\u2192        }\n    40\u2192    )\n    41\u2192    def test_querystring_remove_all_params_custom_querydict(self):\n    42\u2192        context = {\"my_query_dict\": QueryDict(\"a=1&b=2\"), \"my_dict\": {\"b\": None}}\n    43\u2192        self.assertRenderEqual(\n    44\u2192            \"querystring_remove_all_params_custom_querydict\", Context,\n     7\u2192    Engine,\n     8\u2192    RequestContext,\n     9\u2192    Template,\n    10\u2192    Variable,\n    11\u2192    VariableDoesNotExist,\n    12\u2192)\n    13\u2192from django.template.context import RenderContext\n    14\u2192from django.test import RequestFactory, SimpleTestCase, override_settings\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192class ContextTests(SimpleTestCase):\n    18\u2192    def test_context(self):\n    19\u2192        c = Context({\"a\": 1, \"b\": \"xyzzy\"})\n    20\u2192        self.assertEqual(c[\"a\"], 1)\n    21\u2192        self.assertEqual(c.push(), {})\n    22\u2192        c[\"a\"] = 2\n    23\u2192        self.assertEqual(c[\"a\"], 2)\n    24\u2192        self.assertEqual(c.get(\"a\"), 2)\n    25\u2192        self.assertEqual(c.pop(), {\"a\": 2})\n    26\u2192        self.assertEqual(c[\"a\"], 1)\n    27\u2192        self.assertEqual(c.get(\"foo\", 42), 42)\n    28\u2192        self.assertEqual(c, mock.ANY)\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192    def test_push_context_manager(self):\n    31\u2192        c = Context({\"a\": 1})\n    32\u2192        with c.push():\n    33\u2192            c[\"a\"] = 2\n    34\u2192            self.assertEqual(c[\"a\"], 2)\n    35\u2192        self.assertEqual(c[\"a\"], 1)\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192        with c.push(a=3):\n    38\u2192            self.assertEqual(c[\"a\"], 3)\n    39\u2192        self.assertEqual(c[\"a\"], 1)\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192    def test_update_context_manager(self):\n    42\u2192        c = Context({\"a\": 1})\n    43\u2192        with c.update({}):\n    44\u2192            c[\"a\"] = 2\n    45\u2192            self.assertEqual(c[\"a\"], 2)\n    46\u2192        self.assertEqual(c[\"a\"], 1)\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192        with c.update({\"a\": 3}):\n    49\u2192            self.assertEqual(c[\"a\"], 3)\n    50\u2192        self.assertEqual(c[\"a\"], 1)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you This should never receive unrendered content.\n    48\u2192        request.process_response_content = response.content\n    49\u2192        request.process_response_reached = True\n    50\u2192        return response\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. "     1\u2192from unittest import mock\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192from django.template import TemplateDoesNotExist\n     4\u2192from django.test import Client, RequestFactory, SimpleTestCase, override_settings\n     5\u2192from django.utils.translation import override\n     6\u2192from django.views.csrf import CSRF_FAILURE_TEMPLATE_NAME, csrf_failure\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192\n     9\u2192@override_settings(ROOT_URLCONF=\"view_tests.urls\")\n    10\u2192class CsrfViewTests(SimpleTestCase):\n    11\u2192    def setUp(self):\n    12\u2192        super().setUp()\n    13\u2192        self.client = Client(enforce_csrf_checks=True)\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192    @override_settings(\n    16\u2192        USE_I18N=True,\n    17\u2192        MIDDLEWARE=[\n    18\u2192            \"django.middleware.locale.LocaleMiddleware\",\n    19\u2192            \"django.middleware.common.CommonMiddleware\",\n    20\u2192            \"django.middleware.csrf.CsrfViewMiddleware\",\n    21\u2192        ],\n    22\u2192    )\n    23\u2192    def test_translation(self):\n    24\u2192        \"\"\"An invalid request is rejected with a localized error message.\"\"\"\n    25\u2192        response = self.client.post(\"/\")\n    26\u2192        self.assertContains(response, \"Forbidden\", status_code=403)\n    27\u2192        self.assertContains(\n    28\u2192            response, \"CSRF verification failed. Request aborted.\", status_code=403\n    29\u2192        )\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192        with self.settings(LANGUAGE_CODE=\"nl\"), override(\"en-us\"):\n    32\u2192            response = self.client.post(\"/\")\n    33\u2192            self.assertContains(response, \"Verboden\", status_code=403)\n    34\u2192            self.assertContains(\n    35\u2192                response,\n    36\u2192                \"CSRF-verificatie mislukt. Verzoek afgebroken.\",\n    37\u2192                status_code=403,\n    38\u2192            )\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192    @override_settings(SECURE_PROXY_SSL_HEADER=(\"HTTP_X_FORWARDED_PROTO\", \"https\"))\n    41\u2192    def test_no_referer(self):\n    42\u2192        \"\"\"\n    43\u2192        Referer header is strictly checked for POST over HTTPS. Trigger the\n    44\u2192        exception by sending an incorrect referer.\n    45\u2192        \"\"\"\n    46\u2192        response = self.client.post(\"/\", headers={\"x-forwarded-proto\": \"https\"})\n    47\u2192        self.assertContains(\n    48\u2192            response,\n    49\u2192            \"You are seeing this message because this HTTPS site requires a \"\n    50\u2192            \"\u201cReferer header\u201d to be sent by your web browser, but \"\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. django.test.client import RequestFactory\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192from .test_views import AuthViewsTestCase\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192@override_settings(ROOT_URLCONF=\"auth_tests.urls\")\n    20\u2192class LoginRequiredTestCase(AuthViewsTestCase):\n    21\u2192    \"\"\"\n    22\u2192    Tests the login_required decorators\n    23\u2192    \"\"\"\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192    factory = RequestFactory()\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192    def test_wrapped_sync_function_is_not_coroutine_function(self):\n    28\u2192        def sync_view(request):\n    29\u2192            return HttpResponse()\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192        wrapped_view = login_required(sync_view)\n    32\u2192        self.assertIs(iscoroutinefunction(wrapped_view), False)\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192    def test_wrapped_async_function_is_coroutine_function(self):\n    35\u2192        async def async_view(request):\n    36\u2192            return HttpResponse()\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192        wrapped_view = login_required(async_view)\n    39\u2192        self.assertIs(iscoroutinefunction(wrapped_view), True)\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192    def test_callable(self):\n    42\u2192        \"\"\"\n    43\u2192        login_required is assignable to callable objects.\n    44\u2192        \"\"\"\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192        class CallableView:\n    47\u2192            def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):\n    48\u2192                pass\n "     1\u2192from django.apps import apps\n     2\u2192from django.contrib.auth import authenticate, signals\n     3\u2192from django.contrib.auth.models import User\n     4\u2192from django.core.exceptions import FieldDoesNotExist\n     5\u2192from django.test import TestCase, override_settings\n     6\u2192from django.test.client import RequestFactory\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192from .models import MinimalUser, UserWithDisabledLastLoginField\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192@override_settings(ROOT_URLCONF=\"auth_tests.urls\")\n    12\u2192class SignalTestCase(TestCase):\n    13\u2192    @classmethod\n    14\u2192    def setUpTestData(cls):\n    15\u2192        cls.u1 = User.objects.create_user(username=\"testclient\", password=\"password\")\n    16\u2192        cls.u3 = User.objects.create_user(username=\"staff\", password=\"password\")\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192    def listener_login(self, user, **kwargs):\n    19\u2192        self.logged_in.append(user)\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192    def listener_logout(self, user, **kwargs):\n    22\u2192        self.logged_out.append(user)\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192    def listener_login_failed(self, sender, **kwargs):\n    25\u2192        self.login_failed.append(kwargs)\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192    def setUp(self):\n    28\u2192        \"\"\"Set up the listeners and reset the logged in/logged out counters\"\"\"\n    29\u2192        self.logged_in = []\n    30\u2192        self.logged_out = []\n    31\u2192        self.login_failed = []\n    32\u2192        signals.user_logged_in.connect(self.listener_login)\n    33\u2192        self.addCleanup(signals.user_logged_in.disconnect, self.listener_login)\n    34\u2192        signals.user_logged_out.connect(self.listener_logout)\n    35\u2192        self.addCleanup(signals.user_logged_out.disconnect, self.listener_logout)\n    36\u2192        signals.user_login_failed.connect(self.listener_login_failed)\n    37\u2192        self.addCleanup(\n    38\u2192            signals.user_login_failed.disconnect, self.listener_login_failed\n    39\u2192        )\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192    def test_login(self):\n    42\u2192        # Only a successful login will trigger the success signal.\n    43\u2192        self.client.login(username=\"testclient\", password=\"bad\")\n    44\u2192        self.assertEqual(len(self.logged_in), 0)\n    45\u2192        self.assertEqual(len(self.login_failed), 1)\n    46\u2192        self.assertEqual(self.login_failed[0][\"credentials\"][\"username\"], \"testclient\")\n    47\u2192        # verify the password is cleansed\n    48\u2192        self.assertIn(\"***\", self.login_failed[0][\"credentials\"][\"password\"])\n    49\u2192        self.assertIn(\"request\", self.login_failed[0])\n    50\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should we need to make this a\n    22\u2192        # no-op so we can still read its contents.\n    23\u2192        pass\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192class WSGIRequestHandlerTestCase(SimpleTestCase):\n    27\u2192    request_factory = RequestFactory()\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192    def test_log_message(self):\n    30\u2192        request = WSGIRequest(self.request_factory.get(\"/\").environ)\n    31\u2192        request.makefile = lambda *args, **kwargs: BytesIO()\n    32\u2192        handler = WSGIRequestHandler(request, \"192.168.0.2\", None)\n    33\u2192        level_status_codes = {\n    34\u2192            \"info\": [200, 301, 304],\n    35\u2192            \"warning\": [400, 403, 404],\n    36\u2192            \"error\": [500, 503],\n    37\u2192        }\n    38\u2192        for level, status_codes in level_status_codes.items():\n    39\u2192            for status_code in status_codes:\n    40\u2192                # The correct level gets the message.\n    41\u2192                with self.assertLogs(\"django.server\", level.upper()) as cm:\n    42\u2192                    handler.log_message(\"GET %s %s\", \"A\", str(status_code))\n    43\u2192                self.assertIn(\"GET A %d\" % status_code, cm.output[0])\n    44\u2192                # Incorrect levels don't have any messages.\n    45\u2192                for wrong_level in level_status_codes:\n    46\u2192                    if wrong_level != level:\n    47\u2192                        with self.assertLogs(\"django.server\", \"INFO\") as cm:\n    48\u2192                            handler.log_message(\"GET %s %s\", \"A\", str(status_code))\n    49\u2192                        self.assertNotEqual(\n    50\u2192                            cm.records[0].levelname, wrong_level.upper()\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. (Django templates)\\n\")\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192    def test_get_template_using_engine(self):\n    37\u2192        template = get_template(\"template_loader/hello.html\", using=\"django\")\n    38\u2192        self.assertEqual(template.render(), \"Hello! charset=utf-8\", REQUEST_METHOD=\"GET\"\n    27\u2192        )\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192        response_data = {}\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192        def start_response(status, headers):\n    32\u2192            response_data[\"status\"] = status\n    33\u2192            response_data[\"headers\"] = headers\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192        response = application(environ, start_response)\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192        self.assertEqual(response_data[\"status\"], \"200 OK\")\n    38\u2192        self.assertEqual(\n    39\u2192            set(response_data[\"headers\"]),\n    40\u2192            {(\"Content-Length\", \"12\"), (\"Content-Type\", \"text/html; self.assertEqual(expected, actual, \"can_delete must be equal\")\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192    def test_readonly_stacked_inline_label(self):\n   113\u2192        \"\"\"Bug #13174.\"\"\"\n   114\u2192 parent=parent)\n   124\u2192        response = self.client.get(\n   125\u2192            reverse(\"admin:admin_inlines_uuidparent_change\", args=(parent.id,))\n   126\u2192        )\n   127\u2192 many-to-many inlines are displayed correctly (#13407)\"\n   136\u2192        response = self.client.get(reverse(\"admin:admin_inlines_author_add\"))\n   137\u2192        # The heading for the m2m inline block uses the right text\n   138\u2192        self.assertContains(\n   139\u2192            response,\n   140\u2192            (\n   141\u2192                '<h2 id=\"Author_books-heading\" class=\"inline-heading\">'\n   142\u2192                \"Author-book relationships</h2>\"\n   143\u2192            ),\n   144\u2192            html=True,\n   145\u2192        )\n   146\u2192        # The \"add another\" label is correct\n   147\u2192        self.assertContains(response, \"Add another Author-book relationship\")\n   148\u2192        # The '+' is dropped from the autogenerated form prefix (Author_books+)\n   149\u2192        self.assertContains(response, 'id=\"id_Author_books-TOTAL_FORMS\"')\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192    def test_inline_primary(self):\n   152\u2192        person = Person.objects.create(firstname=\"Imelda\")\n   153\u2192        item = OutfitItem.objects.create(name=\"Shoes\")\n   154\u2192        # Imelda likes shoes, but can't carry her own bags.\n   155\u2192        data = {\n   156\u2192            \"shoppingweakness_set-TOTAL_FORMS\": 1,\n   157\u2192            \"shoppingweakness_set-INITIAL_FORMS\": 0,\n   158\u2192 data\n   166\u2192        )\n   167\u2192        self.assertEqual(response.status_code, 302)\n   168\u2192        self.assertEqual(len(Fashionista.objects.filter(person__firstname=\"Imelda\")), 1)\n 211. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/tests/asgi/tests.py",
  "new_string": "from django.test import (\n    AsyncRequestFactory,\n    SimpleTestCase,\n    ignore_warnings,\n    modify_settings,",
  "old_string": "from django.test import (\n    AsyncTestRequestBuilder,\n    SimpleTestCase,\n    ignore_warnings,\n    modify_settings,",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/tests/asgi/tests.py",
  "newString": "from django.test import (\n    AsyncRequestFactory,\n    SimpleTestCase,\n    ignore_warnings,\n    modify_settings,",
  "oldString": "from django.test import (\n    AsyncTestRequestBuilder,\n    SimpleTestCase,\n    ignore_warnings,\n    modify_settings,",
  "originalFile": "import asyncio\nimport sys\nimport tempfile\nimport threading\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom unittest.mock import patch\n\nfrom asgiref.sync import sync_to_async\nfrom asgiref.testing import ApplicationCommunicator\n\nfrom django.contrib.staticfiles.handlers import ASGIStaticFilesHandler\nfrom django.core.asgi import get_asgi_application\nfrom django.core.exceptions import RequestDataTooBig\nfrom django.core.handlers.asgi import ASGIHandler, ASGIRequest\nfrom django.core.signals import request_finished, request_started\nfrom django.db import close_old_connections\nfrom django.http import HttpResponse, StreamingHttpResponse\nfrom django.test import (\n    AsyncTestRequestBuilder,\n    SimpleTestCase,\n    ignore_warnings,\n    modify_settings,\n    override_settings,\n)\nfrom django.test.utils import captured_stderr\nfrom django.urls import path\nfrom django.utils.http import http_date\nfrom django.views.decorators.csrf import csrf_exempt\n\nfrom .urls import sync_waiter, test_filename\n\nTEST_STATIC_ROOT = Path(__file__).parent / \"project\" / \"static\"\n\n\nclass SignalHandler:\n    \"\"\"Helper class to track threads and kwargs when signals are dispatched.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        super().__init__()\n        self.calls = []\n\n    def __call__(self, signal, **kwargs):\n        self.calls.append({\"thread\": threading.current_thread(), \"kwargs\": kwargs})\n\n\n@override_settings(ROOT_URLCONF=\"asgi.urls\")\nclass ASGITest(SimpleTestCase):\n    async_request_factory = AsyncTestRequestBuilder()\n\n    def setUp(self):\n        request_started.disconnect(close_old_connections)\n        self.addCleanup(request_started.connect, close_old_connections)\n\n    async def test_get_asgi_application(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        get_asgi_application() returns a functioning ASGI callable.\n        \"\"\"\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        # Construct HTTP request.\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        # Read the response.\n        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n        self.assertEqual(\n            set(response_start[\"headers\"]),\n            {\n                (b\"Content-Length\", b\"12\"),\n                (b\"Content-Type\", b\"text/html; charset=utf-8\"),\n            },\n        )\n        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"Hello World!\")\n        # Allow response.close() to finish.\n        await communicator.wait()\n\n    async def test_asgi_cookies(self):\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/cookie/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertIn((b\"Set-Cookie\", b\"key=value; Path=/\"), response_start[\"headers\"])\n        # Allow response.close() to finish.\n        await communicator.wait()\n\n    # Python's file API is not async compatible. A third-party library such\n    # as https://github.com/Tinche/aiofiles allows passing the file to\n    # FileResponse as an async iterator. With a sync iterator\n    # StreamingHTTPResponse triggers a warning when iterating the file.\n    # assertWarnsMessage is not async compatible, so ignore_warnings for the\n    # test.\n    @ignore_warnings(module=\"django.core.handlers.asgi\")\n    async def test_file_response(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Makes sure that FileResponse works over ASGI.\n        \"\"\"\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        # Construct HTTP request.\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/file/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        # Get the file content.\n        with open(test_filename, \"rb\") as test_file:\n            test_file_contents = test_file.read()\n        # Read the response.\n        with captured_stderr():\n            response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n        headers = response_start[\"headers\"]\n        self.assertEqual(len(headers), 3)\n        expected_headers = {\n            b\"Content-Length\": str(len(test_file_contents)).encode(\"ascii\"),\n            b\"Content-Type\": b\"text/x-python\",\n            b\"Content-Disposition\": b'inline; filename=\"urls.py\"',\n        }\n        for key, value in headers:\n            try:\n                self.assertEqual(value, expected_headers[key])\n            except AssertionError:\n                # Windows registry may not be configured with correct\n                # mimetypes.\n                if sys.platform == \"win32\" and key == b\"Content-Type\":\n                    self.assertEqual(value, b\"text/plain\")\n                else:\n                    raise\n\n        # Warning ignored here.\n        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], test_file_contents)\n        # Allow response.close() to finish.\n        await communicator.wait()\n\n    @modify_settings(INSTALLED_APPS={\"append\": \"django.contrib.staticfiles\"})\n    @override_settings(\n        STATIC_URL=\"static/\",\n        STATIC_ROOT=TEST_STATIC_ROOT,\n        STATICFILES_DIRS=[TEST_STATIC_ROOT],\n        STATICFILES_FINDERS=[\n            \"django.contrib.staticfiles.finders.FileSystemFinder\",\n        ],\n    )\n    async def test_static_file_response(self):\n        application = ASGIStaticFilesHandler(get_asgi_application())\n        # Construct HTTP request.\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/static/file.txt\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        # Get the file content.\n        file_path = TEST_STATIC_ROOT / \"file.txt\"\n        with open(file_path, \"rb\") as test_file:\n            test_file_contents = test_file.read()\n        # Read the response.\n        stat = file_path.stat()\n        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n        self.assertEqual(\n            set(response_start[\"headers\"]),\n            {\n                (b\"Content-Length\", str(len(test_file_contents)).encode(\"ascii\")),\n                (b\"Content-Type\", b\"text/plain\"),\n                (b\"Content-Disposition\", b'inline; filename=\"file.txt\"'),\n                (b\"Last-Modified\", http_date(stat.st_mtime).encode(\"ascii\")),\n            },\n        )\n        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], test_file_contents)\n        # Allow response.close() to finish.\n        await communicator.wait()\n\n    async def test_headers(self):\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(\n            application,\n            self.async_request_factory._base_scope(\n                path=\"/meta/\",\n                headers=[\n                    [b\"content-type\", b\"text/plain; charset=utf-8\"],\n                    [b\"content-length\", b\"77\"],\n                    [b\"referer\", b\"Scotland\"],\n                    [b\"referer\", b\"Wales\"],\n                ],\n            ),\n        )\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n        self.assertEqual(\n            set(response_start[\"headers\"]),\n            {\n                (b\"Content-Length\", b\"19\"),\n                (b\"Content-Type\", b\"text/plain; charset=utf-8\"),\n            },\n        )\n        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"From Scotland,Wales\")\n        # Allow response.close() to finish\n        await communicator.wait()\n\n    async def test_post_body(self):\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(\n            method=\"POST\",\n            path=\"/post/\",\n            query_string=\"echo=1\",\n        )\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"Echo!\"})\n        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"Echo!\")\n\n    async def test_create_request_error(self):\n        # Track request_finished signal.\n        signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n        request_finished.connect(signal_handler)\n        self.addCleanup(request_finished.disconnect, signal_handler)\n\n        # Request class that always fails creation with RequestDataTooBig.\n        class TestASGIRequest(ASGIRequest):\n\n            def __init__(self, scope, body_file):\n                super().__init__(scope, body_file)\n                raise RequestDataTooBig()\n\n        # Handler to use the custom request class.\n        class TestASGIHandler(ASGIHandler):\n            request_class = TestASGIRequest\n\n        application = TestASGIHandler()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/not-important/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n\n        # Initiate request.\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        # Give response.close() time to finish.\n        await communicator.wait()\n\n        self.assertEqual(len(signal_handler.calls), 1)\n        self.assertNotEqual(\n            signal_handler.calls[0][\"thread\"], threading.current_thread()\n        )\n\n    async def test_cancel_post_request_with_sync_processing(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        The request.body object should be available and readable in view\n        code, even if the ASGIHandler cancels processing part way through.\n        \"\"\"\n        loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()\n        # Events to monitor the view processing from the parent test code.\n        view_started_event = asyncio.Event()\n        view_finished_event = asyncio.Event()\n        # Record received request body or exceptions raised in the test view\n        outcome = []\n\n        # This view will run in a new thread because it is wrapped in\n        # sync_to_async. The view consumes the POST body data after a short\n        # delay. The test will cancel the request using http.disconnect during\n        # the delay, but because this is a sync view the code runs to\n        # completion. There should be no exceptions raised inside the view\n        # code.\n        @csrf_exempt\n        @sync_to_async\n        def post_view(request):\n            try:\n                loop.call_soon_threadsafe(view_started_event.set)\n                time.sleep(0.1)\n                # Do something to read request.body after pause\n                outcome.append({\"request_body\": request.body})\n                return HttpResponse(\"ok\")\n            except Exception as e:\n                outcome.append({\"exception\": e})\n            finally:\n                loop.call_soon_threadsafe(view_finished_event.set)\n\n        # Request class to use the view.\n        class TestASGIRequest(ASGIRequest):\n            urlconf = (path(\"post/\", post_view),)\n\n        # Handler to use request class.\n        class TestASGIHandler(ASGIHandler):\n            request_class = TestASGIRequest\n\n        application = TestASGIHandler()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(\n            method=\"POST\",\n            path=\"/post/\",\n        )\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"Body data!\"})\n\n        # Wait until the view code has started, then send http.disconnect.\n        await view_started_event.wait()\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n        # Wait until view code has finished.\n        await view_finished_event.wait()\n        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n            await communicator.receive_output()\n\n        self.assertEqual(outcome, [{\"request_body\": b\"Body data!\"}])\n\n    async def test_untouched_request_body_gets_closed(self):\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(method=\"POST\", path=\"/post/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 204)\n        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"\")\n        # Allow response.close() to finish\n        await communicator.wait()\n\n    async def test_get_query_string(self):\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        for query_string in (b\"name=Andrew\", \"name=Andrew\"):\n            with self.subTest(query_string=query_string):\n                scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(\n                    path=\"/\",\n                    query_string=query_string,\n                )\n                communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n                await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n                response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n                self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n                self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n                response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n                self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n                self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"Hello Andrew!\")\n                # Allow response.close() to finish\n                await communicator.wait()\n\n    async def test_disconnect(self):\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n            await communicator.receive_output()\n\n    async def test_disconnect_both_return(self):\n        # Force both the disconnect listener and the task that sends the\n        # response to finish at the same time.\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"some body\"})\n        # Fetch response headers (this yields to asyncio and causes\n        # ASGHandler.send_response() to dump the body of the response in the\n        # queue).\n        await communicator.receive_output()\n        # Fetch response body (there's already some data queued up, so this\n        # doesn't actually yield to the event loop, it just succeeds\n        # instantly).\n        await communicator.receive_output()\n        # Send disconnect at the same time that response finishes (this just\n        # puts some info in a queue, it doesn't have to yield to the event\n        # loop).\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n        # Waiting for the communicator _does_ yield to the event loop, since\n        # ASGIHandler.send_response() is still waiting to do response.close().\n        # It so happens that there are enough remaining yield points in both\n        # tasks that they both finish while the loop is running.\n        await communicator.wait()\n\n    async def test_disconnect_with_body(self):\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"some body\"})\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n            await communicator.receive_output()\n\n    async def test_assert_in_listen_for_disconnect(self):\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.not_a_real_message\"})\n        msg = \"Invalid ASGI message after request body: http.not_a_real_message\"\n        with self.assertRaisesMessage(AssertionError, msg):\n            await communicator.wait()\n\n    async def test_delayed_disconnect_with_body(self):\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/delayed_hello/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"some body\"})\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n            await communicator.receive_output()\n\n    async def test_wrong_connection_type(self):\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\", type=\"other\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        msg = \"Django can only handle ASGI/HTTP connections, not other.\"\n        with self.assertRaisesMessage(ValueError, msg):\n            await communicator.receive_output()\n\n    async def test_non_unicode_query_string(self):\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\", query_string=b\"\\xff\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 400)\n        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"\")\n\n    async def test_request_lifecycle_signals_dispatched_with_thread_sensitive(self):\n        # Track request_started and request_finished signals.\n        signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n        request_started.connect(signal_handler)\n        self.addCleanup(request_started.disconnect, signal_handler)\n        request_finished.connect(signal_handler)\n        self.addCleanup(request_finished.disconnect, signal_handler)\n\n        # Perform a basic request.\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"Hello World!\")\n        # Give response.close() time to finish.\n        await communicator.wait()\n\n        # AsyncToSync should have executed the signals in the same thread.\n        self.assertEqual(len(signal_handler.calls), 2)\n        request_started_call, request_finished_call = signal_handler.calls\n        self.assertEqual(\n            request_started_call[\"thread\"], request_finished_call[\"thread\"]\n        )\n\n    async def test_concurrent_async_uses_multiple_thread_pools(self):\n        sync_waiter.active_threads.clear()\n\n        # Send 2 requests concurrently\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/wait/\")\n        communicators = []\n        for _ in range(2):\n            communicators.append(ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope))\n            await communicators[-1].send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n\n        # Each request must complete with a status code of 200\n        # If requests aren't scheduled concurrently, the barrier in the\n        # sync_wait view will time out, resulting in a 500 status code.\n        for communicator in communicators:\n            response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n            self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n            self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n            response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n            self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n            self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"Hello World!\")\n            # Give response.close() time to finish.\n            await communicator.wait()\n\n        # The requests should have scheduled on different threads. Note\n        # active_threads is a set (a thread can only appear once), therefore\n        # length is a sufficient check.\n        self.assertEqual(len(sync_waiter.active_threads), 2)\n\n        sync_waiter.active_threads.clear()\n\n    async def test_asyncio_cancel_error(self):\n        view_started = asyncio.Event()\n        # Flag to check if the view was cancelled.\n        view_did_cancel = False\n        # Track request_finished signal.\n        signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n        request_finished.connect(signal_handler)\n        self.addCleanup(request_finished.disconnect, signal_handler)\n\n        # A view that will listen for the cancelled error.\n        async def view(request):\n            nonlocal view_did_cancel\n            view_started.set()\n            try:\n                await asyncio.sleep(0.1)\n                return HttpResponse(\"Hello World!\")\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                # Set the flag.\n                view_did_cancel = True\n                raise\n\n        # Request class to use the view.\n        class TestASGIRequest(ASGIRequest):\n            urlconf = (path(\"cancel/\", view),)\n\n        # Handler to use request class.\n        class TestASGIHandler(ASGIHandler):\n            request_class = TestASGIRequest\n\n        # Request cycle should complete since no disconnect was sent.\n        application = TestASGIHandler()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/cancel/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"Hello World!\")\n        # Give response.close() time to finish.\n        await communicator.wait()\n        self.assertIs(view_did_cancel, False)\n        # Exactly one call to request_finished handler.\n        self.assertEqual(len(signal_handler.calls), 1)\n        handler_call = signal_handler.calls.pop()\n        # It was NOT on the async thread.\n        self.assertNotEqual(handler_call[\"thread\"], threading.current_thread())\n        # The signal sender is the handler class.\n        self.assertEqual(handler_call[\"kwargs\"], {\"sender\": TestASGIHandler})\n        view_started.clear()\n\n        # Request cycle with a disconnect before the view can respond.\n        application = TestASGIHandler()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/cancel/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        # Let the view actually start.\n        await view_started.wait()\n        # Disconnect the client.\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n        # The handler should not send a response.\n        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n            await communicator.receive_output()\n        await communicator.wait()\n        self.assertIs(view_did_cancel, True)\n        # Exactly one call to request_finished handler.\n        self.assertEqual(len(signal_handler.calls), 1)\n        handler_call = signal_handler.calls.pop()\n        # It was NOT on the async thread.\n        self.assertNotEqual(handler_call[\"thread\"], threading.current_thread())\n        # The signal sender is the handler class.\n        self.assertEqual(handler_call[\"kwargs\"], {\"sender\": TestASGIHandler})\n\n    async def test_asyncio_streaming_cancel_error(self):\n        # Similar to test_asyncio_cancel_error(), but during a streaming\n        # response.\n        view_did_cancel = False\n        # Track request_finished signals.\n        signal_handler = SignalHandler()\n        request_finished.connect(signal_handler)\n        self.addCleanup(request_finished.disconnect, signal_handler)\n\n        async def streaming_response():\n            nonlocal view_did_cancel\n            try:\n                await asyncio.sleep(0.2)\n                yield b\"Hello World!\"\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                # Set the flag.\n                view_did_cancel = True\n                raise\n\n        async def view(request):\n            return StreamingHttpResponse(streaming_response())\n\n        class TestASGIRequest(ASGIRequest):\n            urlconf = (path(\"cancel/\", view),)\n\n        class TestASGIHandler(ASGIHandler):\n            request_class = TestASGIRequest\n\n        # With no disconnect, the request cycle should complete in the same\n        # manner as the non-streaming response.\n        application = TestASGIHandler()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/cancel/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n        response_body = await communicator.receive_output()\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"type\"], \"http.response.body\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_body[\"body\"], b\"Hello World!\")\n        await communicator.wait()\n        self.assertIs(view_did_cancel, False)\n        # Exactly one call to request_finished handler.\n        self.assertEqual(len(signal_handler.calls), 1)\n        handler_call = signal_handler.calls.pop()\n        # It was NOT on the async thread.\n        self.assertNotEqual(handler_call[\"thread\"], threading.current_thread())\n        # The signal sender is the handler class.\n        self.assertEqual(handler_call[\"kwargs\"], {\"sender\": TestASGIHandler})\n\n        # Request cycle with a disconnect.\n        application = TestASGIHandler()\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(path=\"/cancel/\")\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        response_start = await communicator.receive_output()\n        # Fetch the start of response so streaming can begin\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"type\"], \"http.response.start\")\n        self.assertEqual(response_start[\"status\"], 200)\n        await asyncio.sleep(0.1)\n        # Now disconnect the client.\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n        # This time the handler should not send a response.\n        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n            await communicator.receive_output()\n        await communicator.wait()\n        self.assertIs(view_did_cancel, True)\n        # Exactly one call to request_finished handler.\n        self.assertEqual(len(signal_handler.calls), 1)\n        handler_call = signal_handler.calls.pop()\n        # It was NOT on the async thread.\n        self.assertNotEqual(handler_call[\"thread\"], threading.current_thread())\n        # The signal sender is the handler class.\n        self.assertEqual(handler_call[\"kwargs\"], {\"sender\": TestASGIHandler})\n\n    async def test_streaming(self):\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(\n            path=\"/streaming/\", query_string=b\"sleep=0.001\"\n        )\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        # Fetch http.response.start.\n        await communicator.receive_output(timeout=1)\n        # Fetch the 'first' and 'last'.\n        first_response = await communicator.receive_output(timeout=1)\n        self.assertEqual(first_response[\"body\"], b\"first\\n\")\n        second_response = await communicator.receive_output(timeout=1)\n        self.assertEqual(second_response[\"body\"], b\"last\\n\")\n        # Fetch the rest of the response so that coroutines are cleaned up.\n        await communicator.receive_output(timeout=1)\n        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n            await communicator.receive_output(timeout=1)\n\n    async def test_streaming_disconnect(self):\n        scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(\n            path=\"/streaming/\", query_string=b\"sleep=0.1\"\n        )\n        application = get_asgi_application()\n        communicator = ApplicationCommunicator(application, scope)\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.request\"})\n        await communicator.receive_output(timeout=1)\n        first_response = await communicator.receive_output(timeout=1)\n        self.assertEqual(first_response[\"body\"], b\"first\\n\")\n        # Disconnect the client.\n        await communicator.send_input({\"type\": \"http.disconnect\"})\n        # 'last\\n' isn't sent.\n        with self.assertRaises(asyncio.TimeoutError):\n            await communicator.receive_output(timeout=0.2)\n\n    async def test_read_body_thread(self):\n        \"\"\"Write runs on correct thread depending on rollover.\"\"\"\n        handler = ASGIHandler()\n        loop_thread = threading.current_thread()\n\n        called_threads = []\n\n        def write_wrapper(data):\n            called_threads.append(threading.current_thread())\n            return original_write(data)\n\n        # In-memory write (no rollover expected).\n        in_memory_chunks = [\n            {\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"small\", \"more_body\": False}\n        ]\n\n        async def receive():\n            return in_memory_chunks.pop(0)\n\n        with tempfile.SpooledTemporaryFile(max_size=1024, mode=\"w+b\") as temp_file:\n            original_write = temp_file.write\n            with (\n                patch(\n                    \"django.core.handlers.asgi.tempfile.SpooledTemporaryFile\",\n                    return_value=temp_file,\n                ),\n                patch.object(temp_file, \"write\", side_effect=write_wrapper),\n            ):\n                await handler.read_body(receive)\n        # Write was called in the event loop thread.\n        self.assertIn(loop_thread, called_threads)\n\n        # Clear thread log before next test.\n        called_threads.clear()\n\n        # Rollover to disk (write should occur in a threadpool thread).\n        rolled_chunks = [\n            {\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"A\" * 16, \"more_body\": True},\n            {\"type\": \"http.request\", \"body\": b\"B\" * 16, \"more_body\": False},\n        ]\n\n        async def receive_rolled():\n            return rolled_chunks.pop(0)\n\n        with (\n            override_settings(FILE_UPLOAD_MAX_MEMORY_SIZE=10),\n            tempfile.SpooledTemporaryFile(max_size=10, mode=\"w+b\") as temp_file,\n        ):\n            original_write = temp_file.write\n            # roll_over force in handlers.\n            with (\n                patch(\n                    \"django.core.handlers.asgi.tempfile.SpooledTemporaryFile\",\n                    return_value=temp_file,\n                ),\n                patch.object(temp_file, \"write\", side_effect=write_wrapper),\n            ):\n                await handler.read_body(receive_rolled)\n        # The second write should have rolled over to disk.\n        self.assertTrue(any(t != loop_thread for t in called_threads))\n\n    def test_multiple_cookie_headers_http2(self):\n        test_cases = [\n            {\n                \"label\": \"RFC-compliant headers (no semicolon)\",\n                \"headers\": [\n                    (b\"cookie\", b\"a=abc\"),\n                    (b\"cookie\", b\"b=def\"),\n                    (b\"cookie\", b\"c=ghi\"),\n                ],\n            },\n            {\n                # Some clients may send cookies with trailing semicolons.\n                \"label\": \"Headers with trailing semicolons\",\n                \"headers\": [\n                    (b\"cookie\", b\"a=abc;\"),\n                    (b\"cookie\", b\"b=def;\"),\n                    (b\"cookie\", b\"c=ghi;\"),\n                ],\n            },\n        ]\n\n        for case in test_cases:\n            with self.subTest(case[\"label\"]):\n                scope = self.async_request_factory._base_scope(\n                    path=\"/\", http_version=\"2.0\"\n                )\n                scope[\"headers\"] = case[\"headers\"]\n                request = ASGIRequest(scope, None)\n                self.assertEqual(request.META[\"HTTP_COOKIE\"], \"a=abc; b=def; c=ghi\")\n                self.assertEqual(request.COOKIES, {\"a\": \"abc\", \"b\": \"def\", \"c\": \"ghi\"})\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
