USE_TZ=True)\n    def test_cache_key_i18n_timezone(self):\n        request = self.factory.get(self.path)\n        tz = timezone.get_current_timezone_name()\n        response = HttpResponse()\n        key = learn_cache_key(request, response)\n        self.assertIn(\n            tz,\n            key,\n            \"Cache keys should include the time zone name when time zones are active\",\n        )\n        key2 = get_cache_key(request)\n        self.assertEqual(key, key2)\n\n    @override_settings(USE_I18N=False)\n    def test_cache_key_no_i18n(self):\n        request = self.factory.get(self.path)\n        lang = translation.get_language()\n        tz = timezone.get_current_timezone_name()\n        response = HttpResponse()\n        key = learn_cache_key(request, response)\n        self.assertNotIn(\n            lang,\n            key,\n            \"Cache keys shouldn't include the language name when i18n isn't active\",\n        )\n        self.assertNotIn(\n            tz,\n            key,\n            \"Cache keys shouldn't include the time zone name when i18n isn't active\",\n        )\n\n    @override_settings(\n        CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_KEY_PREFIX=\"test\",\n        CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_SECONDS=60,\n        USE_I18N=True,\n    )\n    def test_middleware(self):\n        def set_cache(request, lang, msg):\n            def get_response(req):\n                return HttpResponse(msg)\n\n            translation.activate(lang)\n            return UpdateCacheMiddleware(get_response)(request)\n\n        # cache with non empty request.GET\n        request = self.factory.get(self.path, {\"foo\": \"bar\", \"other\": \"true\"})\n        request._cache_update_cache = True\n\n        get_cache_data = FetchFromCacheMiddleware(empty_response).process_request(\n            request\n        )\n        # first access, cache must return None\n        self.assertIsNone(get_cache_data)\n        content = \"Check for cache with QUERY_STRING\"\n\n        def get_response(req):\n            return HttpResponse(content)\n\n        UpdateCacheMiddleware(get_response)(request)\n        get_cache_data = FetchFromCacheMiddleware(empty_response).process_request(\n            request\n        )\n        # cache must return content\n        self.assertIsNotNone(get_cache_data)\n        self.assertEqual(get_cache_data.content, content.encode())\n        # different QUERY_STRING, cache must be empty\n        request = self.factory.get(self.path, {\"foo\": \"bar\", \"somethingelse\": \"true\"})\n        request._cache_update_cache = True\n        get_cache_data = FetchFromCacheMiddleware(empty_response).process_request(\n            request\n        )\n        self.assertIsNone(get_cache_data)\n\n        # i18n tests\n        en_message = \"Hello world!\"\n        es_message = \"Hola mundo!\"\n\n        request = self.factory.get(self.path)\n        request._cache_update_cache = True\n        set_cache(request, \"en\", en_message)\n        get_cache_data = FetchFromCacheMiddleware(empty_response).process_request(\n            request\n        )\n        # The cache can be recovered\n        self.assertIsNotNone(get_cache_data)\n        self.assertEqual(get_cache_data.content, en_message.encode())\n        # change the session language and set content\n        request = self.factory.get(self.path)\n        request._cache_update_cache = True\n        set_cache(request, \"es\", es_message)\n        # change again the language\n        translation.activate(\"en\")\n        # retrieve the content from cache\n        get_cache_data = FetchFromCacheMiddleware(empty_response).process_request(\n            request\n        )\n        self.assertEqual(get_cache_data.content, en_message.encode())\n        # change again the language\n        translation.activate(\"es\")\n        get_cache_data = FetchFromCacheMiddleware(empty_response).process_request(\n            request\n        )\n        self.assertEqual(get_cache_data.content, es_message.encode())\n        # reset the language\n        translation.deactivate()\n\n    @override_settings(\n        CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_KEY_PREFIX=\"test\",\n        CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_SECONDS=60,\n    )\n    def test_middleware_doesnt_cache_streaming_response(self):\n        request = self.factory.get(self.path)\n        get_cache_data = FetchFromCacheMiddleware(empty_response).process_request(\n            request\n        )\n        self.assertIsNone(get_cache_data)\n\n        def get_stream_response(req):\n            return StreamingHttpResponse([\"Check for cache with streaming content.\"])\n\n        UpdateCacheMiddleware(get_stream_response)(request)\n\n        get_cache_data = FetchFromCacheMiddleware(empty_response).process_request(\n            request\n        )\n        self.assertIsNone(get_cache_data)\n\n\n@override_settings(\n    CACHES={\n        \"default\": {\n            \"BACKEND\": \"django.core.cache.backends.locmem.LocMemCache\",\n            \"KEY_PREFIX\": \"cacheprefix\",\n        },\n    },\n)\nclass PrefixedCacheI18nTest(CacheI18nTest):\n    pass\n\n\ndef hello_world_view(request, value):\n    return HttpResponse(\"Hello World %s\" % value)\n\n\ndef csrf_view(request):\n    return HttpResponse(csrf(request)[\"csrf_token\"])\n\n\n@override_settings(\n    CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_ALIAS=\"other\",\n    CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_KEY_PREFIX=\"middlewareprefix\",\n    CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_SECONDS=30,\n    CACHES={\n        \"default\": {\n            \"BACKEND\": \"django.core.cache.backends.locmem.LocMemCache\",\n        },\n        \"other\": {\n            \"BACKEND\": \"django.core.cache.backends.locmem.LocMemCache\",\n            \"LOCATION\": \"other\",\n            \"TIMEOUT\": \"1\",\n        },\n    },\n)\nclass CacheMiddlewareTest(SimpleTestCase):\n    factory = TestRequestBuilder()\n\n    def setUp(self):\n        self.default_cache = caches[\"default\"]\n        self.addCleanup(self.default_cache.clear)\n        self.other_cache = caches[\"other\"]\n        self.addCleanup(self.other_cache.clear)\n\n    def test_constructor(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        The constructor is correctly distinguishing between usage of\n        CacheMiddleware as Middleware vs. usage of CacheMiddleware as view\n        decorator and setting attributes appropriately.\n        \"\"\"\n        # If only one argument is passed in construction, it's being used as\n        # middleware.\n        middleware = CacheMiddleware(empty_response)\n\n        # Now test object attributes against values defined in setUp above\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.cache_timeout, 30)\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.key_prefix, \"middlewareprefix\")\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.cache_alias, \"other\")\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.cache, self.other_cache)\n\n        # If more arguments are being passed in construction, it's being used\n        # as a decorator. First, test with \"defaults\":\n        as_view_decorator = CacheMiddleware(\n            empty_response, cache_alias=None, key_prefix=None\n        )\n\n        self.assertEqual(\n            as_view_decorator.cache_timeout, 30\n        )  # Timeout value for 'default' cache, i.e. 30\n        self.assertEqual(as_view_decorator.key_prefix, \"\")\n        # Value of DEFAULT_CACHE_ALIAS from django.core.cache\n        self.assertEqual(as_view_decorator.cache_alias, \"default\")\n        self.assertEqual(as_view_decorator.cache, self.default_cache)\n\n        # Next, test with custom values:\n        as_view_decorator_with_custom = CacheMiddleware(\n            hello_world_view, cache_timeout=60, cache_alias=\"other\", key_prefix=\"foo\"\n        )\n\n        self.assertEqual(as_view_decorator_with_custom.cache_timeout, 60)\n        self.assertEqual(as_view_decorator_with_custom.key_prefix, \"foo\")\n        self.assertEqual(as_view_decorator_with_custom.cache_alias, \"other\")\n        self.assertEqual(as_view_decorator_with_custom.cache, self.other_cache)\n\n    def test_update_cache_middleware_constructor(self):\n        middleware = UpdateCacheMiddleware(empty_response)\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.cache_timeout, 30)\n        self.assertIsNone(middleware.page_timeout)\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.key_prefix, \"middlewareprefix\")\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.cache_alias, \"other\")\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.cache, self.other_cache)\n\n    def test_fetch_cache_middleware_constructor(self):\n        middleware = FetchFromCacheMiddleware(empty_response)\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.key_prefix, \"middlewareprefix\")\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.cache_alias, \"other\")\n        self.assertEqual(middleware.cache, self.other_cache)\n\n    def test_middleware(self):\n        middleware = CacheMiddleware(hello_world_view)\n        prefix_middleware = CacheMiddleware(hello_world_view, key_prefix=\"prefix1\")\n        timeout_middleware = CacheMiddleware(hello_world_view, cache_timeout=1)\n\n        request = self.factory.get(\"/view/\")\n\n        # Put the request through the request middleware\n        result = middleware.process_request(request)\n        self.assertIsNone(result)\n\n        response = hello_world_view(request, \"1\")\n\n        # Now put the response through the response middleware\n        response = middleware.process_response(request, response)\n\n        # Repeating the request should result in a cache hit\n        result = middleware.process_request(request)\n        self.assertIsNotNone(result)\n        self.assertEqual(result.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n\n        # The same request through a different middleware won't hit\n        result = prefix_middleware.process_request(request)\n        self.assertIsNone(result)\n\n        # The same request with a timeout _will_ hit\n        result = timeout_middleware.process_request(request)\n        self.assertIsNotNone(result)\n        self.assertEqual(result.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n\n    def test_view_decorator(self):\n        # decorate the same view with different cache decorators\n        default_view = cache_page(3)(hello_world_view)\n        default_with_prefix_view = cache_page(3, key_prefix=\"prefix1\")(hello_world_view)\n\n        explicit_default_view = cache_page(3, cache=\"default\")(hello_world_view)\n        explicit_default_with_prefix_view = cache_page(\n            3, cache=\"default\", key_prefix=\"prefix1\"\n        )(hello_world_view)\n\n        other_view = cache_page(1, cache=\"other\")(hello_world_view)\n        other_with_prefix_view = cache_page(1, cache=\"other\", key_prefix=\"prefix2\")(\n            hello_world_view\n        )\n\n        request = self.factory.get(\"/view/\")\n\n        # Request the view once\n        response = default_view(request, \"1\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n\n        # Request again -- hit the cache\n        response = default_view(request, \"2\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n\n        # Requesting the same view with the explicit cache should yield the\n        # same result\n        response = explicit_default_view(request, \"3\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n\n        # Requesting with a prefix will hit a different cache key\n        response = explicit_default_with_prefix_view(request, \"4\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 4\")\n\n        # Hitting the same view again gives a cache hit\n        response = explicit_default_with_prefix_view(request, \"5\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 4\")\n\n        # And going back to the implicit cache will hit the same cache\n        response = default_with_prefix_view(request, \"6\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 4\")\n\n        # Requesting from an alternate cache won't hit cache\n        response = other_view(request, \"7\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 7\")\n\n        # But a repeated hit will hit cache\n        response = other_view(request, \"8\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 7\")\n\n        # And prefixing the alternate cache yields yet another cache entry\n        response = other_with_prefix_view(request, \"9\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 9\")\n\n        # But if we wait a couple of seconds...\n        time.sleep(2)\n\n        # ... the default cache will still hit\n        caches[\"default\"]\n        response = default_view(request, \"11\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n\n        # ... the default cache with a prefix will still hit\n        response = default_with_prefix_view(request, \"12\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 4\")\n\n        # ... the explicit default cache will still hit\n        response = explicit_default_view(request, \"13\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n\n        # ... the explicit default cache with a prefix will still hit\n        response = explicit_default_with_prefix_view(request, \"14\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 4\")\n\n        # .. but a rapidly expiring cache won't hit\n        response = other_view(request, \"15\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 15\")\n\n        # .. even if it has a prefix\n        response = other_with_prefix_view(request, \"16\")\n        self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 16\")\n\n    @retry()\n    def test_cache_page_timeout(self):\n        # Page timeout takes precedence over the \"max-age\" section of the\n        # \"Cache-Control\".\n        tests = [\n            (1, 3),  # max_age < page_timeout.\n            (3, 1),  # max_age > page_timeout.\n        ]\n        for max_age, page_timeout in tests:\n            with self.subTest(max_age=max_age, page_timeout=page_timeout):\n                view = cache_page(timeout=page_timeout)(\n                    cache_control(max_age=max_age)(hello_world_view)\n                )\n                request = self.factory.get(\"/view/\")\n                response = view(request, \"1\")\n                self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n                time.sleep(1)\n                response = view(request, \"2\")\n                self.assertEqual(\n                    response.content,\n                    b\"Hello World 1\" if page_timeout > max_age else b\"Hello World 2\",\n                )\n            cache.clear()\n\n    def test_cache_control_not_cached(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Responses with 'Cache-Control: private/no-cache/no-store' are\n        not cached.\n        \"\"\"\n        for cc in (\"private\", \"no-cache\", \"no-store\"):\n            with self.subTest(cache_control=cc):\n                view_with_cache = cache_page(3)(\n                    cache_control(**{cc: True})(hello_world_view)\n                )\n                request = self.factory.get(\"/view/\")\n                response = view_with_cache(request, \"1\")\n                self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 1\")\n                response = view_with_cache(request, \"2\")\n                self.assertEqual(response.content, b\"Hello World 2\")\n\n    def test_sensitive_cookie_not_cached(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Django must prevent caching of responses that set a user-specific (and\n        maybe security sensitive) cookie in response to a cookie-less request.\n        \"\"\"\n        request = self.factory.get(\"/view/\")\n        csrf_middleware = CsrfViewMiddleware(csrf_view)\n        csrf_middleware.process_view(request, csrf_view, (), {})\n        cache_middleware = CacheMiddleware(csrf_middleware)\n\n        self.assertIsNone(cache_middleware.process_request(request))\n        cache_middleware(request)\n\n        # Inserting a CSRF cookie in a cookie-less request prevented caching.\n        self.assertIsNone(cache_middleware.process_request(request))\n\n    def test_304_response_has_http_caching_headers_but_not_cached(self):\n        original_view = mock.Mock(return_value=HttpResponseNotModified())\n        view = cache_page(2)(original_view)\n        request = self.factory.get(\"/view/\")\n        # The view shouldn't be cached on the second call.\n        view(request).close()\n        response = view(request)\n        response.close()\n        self.assertEqual(original_view.call_count, 2)\n        self.assertIsInstance(response, HttpResponseNotModified)\n        self.assertIn(\"Cache-Control\", response)\n        self.assertIn(\"Expires\", response)\n\n    def test_per_thread(self):\n        \"\"\"The cache instance is different for each thread.\"\"\"\n        thread_caches = []\n        middleware = CacheMiddleware(empty_response)\n\n        def runner():\n            thread_caches.append(middleware.cache)\n\n        for _ in range(2):\n            thread = threading.Thread(target=runner)\n            thread.start()\n            thread.join()\n\n        self.assertIsNot(thread_caches[0], thread_caches[1])\n\n    def test_cache_control_max_age(self):\n        view = cache_page(2)(hello_world_view)\n        request = self.factory.get(\"/view/\")\n\n        # First request. Freshly created response gets returned with no Age\n        # header.\n        with mock.patch.object(\n            time, \"time\", return_value=1468749600\n        ):  # Sun, 17 Jul 2016 10:00:00 GMT\n            response = view(request, 1)\n            response.close()\n            self.assertIn(\"Expires\", response)\n            self.assertEqual(response[\"Expires\"], \"Sun, 17 Jul 2016 10:00:02 GMT\")\n            self.assertIn(\"Cache-Control\", response)\n            self.assertEqual(response[\"Cache-Control\"], \"max-age=2\")\n            self.assertNotIn(\"Age\", response)\n\n        # Second request one second later. Response from the cache gets\n        # returned with an Age header set to 1 (second).\n        with mock.patch.object(\n            time, \"time\", return_value=1468749601\n        ):  # Sun, 17 Jul 2016 10:00:01 GMT\n            response = view(request, 1)\n            response.close()\n            self.assertIn(\"Expires\", response)\n            self.assertEqual(response[\"Expires\"], \"Sun, 17 Jul 2016 10:00:02 GMT\")\n            self.assertIn(\"Cache-Control\", response)\n            self.assertEqual(response[\"Cache-Control\"], \"max-age=2\")\n            self.assertIn(\"Age\", response)\n            self.assertEqual(response[\"Age\"], \"1\")\n\n\n@override_settings(\n    CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_KEY_PREFIX=\"settingsprefix\",\n    CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_SECONDS=1,\n    CACHES={\n        \"default\": {\n            \"BACKEND\": \"django.core.cache.backends.locmem.LocMemCache\",\n        },\n    },\n    USE_I18N=False,\n)\nclass TestWithTemplateResponse(SimpleTestCase):\n    \"\"\"\n    Tests various headers w/ TemplateResponse.\n\n    Most are probably redundant since they manipulate the same object\n    anyway but the ETag header is 'special' because it relies on the\n    content being complete (which is not necessarily always the case\n    with a TemplateResponse)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    path = \"/cache/test/\"\n    factory = TestRequestBuilder()\n\n    def tearDown(self):\n        cache.clear()\n\n    def test_patch_vary_headers(self):\n        headers = (\n            # Initial vary, new headers, resulting vary.\n            (None, (\"Accept-Encoding\",), \"Accept-Encoding\"),\n            (\"Accept-Encoding\", (\"accept-encoding\",), \"Accept-Encoding\"),\n            (\"Accept-Encoding\", (\"ACCEPT-ENCODING\",), \"Accept-Encoding\"),\n            (\"Cookie\", (\"Accept-Encoding\",), \"Cookie, Accept-Encoding\"),\n            (\n                \"Cookie, Accept-Encoding\",\n                (\"Accept-Encoding\",),\n                \"Cookie, Accept-Encoding\",\n            ),\n            (\n                \"Cookie, Accept-Encoding\",\n                (\"Accept-Encoding\", \"cookie\"),\n                \"Cookie, Accept-Encoding\",\n            ),\n            (None, (\"Accept-Encoding\", \"COOKIE\"), \"Accept-Encoding, COOKIE\"),\n            (\n                \"Cookie,     Accept-Encoding\",\n                (\"Accept-Encoding\", \"cookie\"),\n                \"Cookie, Accept-Encoding\",\n            ),\n            (\n                \"Cookie    ,     Accept-Encoding\",\n                (\"Accept-Encoding\", \"cookie\"),\n                \"Cookie, Accept-Encoding\",\n            ),\n        )\n        for initial_vary, newheaders, resulting_vary in headers:\n            with self.subTest(initial_vary=initial_vary, newheaders=newheaders):\n                template = engines[\"django\"].from_string(\"This is a test\")\n                response = TemplateResponse(HttpRequest(), template)\n                if initial_vary is not None:\n                    response.headers[\"Vary\"] = initial_vary\n                patch_vary_headers(response, newheaders)\n                self.assertEqual(response.headers[\"Vary\"], resulting_vary)\n\n    def test_get_cache_key(self):\n        request = self.factory.get(self.path)\n        template = engines[\"django\"].from_string(\"This is a test\")\n        response = TemplateResponse(HttpRequest(), template)\n        key_prefix = \"localprefix\"\n        # Expect None if no headers have been set yet.\n        self.assertIsNone(get_cache_key(request))\n        # Set headers to an empty list.\n        learn_cache_key(request, response)\n\n        self.assertEqual(\n            get_cache_key(request),\n            \"views.decorators.cache.cache_page.settingsprefix.GET.\"\n            \"58a0a05c8a5620f813686ff969c26853.d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e\",\n        )\n        # A specified key_prefix is taken into account.\n        learn_cache_key(request, response, key_prefix=key_prefix)\n        self.assertEqual(\n            get_cache_key(request, key_prefix=key_prefix),\n            \"views.decorators.cache.cache_page.localprefix.GET.\"\n            \"58a0a05c8a5620f813686ff969c26853.d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e\",\n        )\n\n    def test_get_cache_key_with_query(self):\n        request = self.factory.get(self.path, {\"test\": 1})\n        template = engines[\"django\"].from_string(\"This is a test\")\n        response = TemplateResponse(HttpRequest(), template)\n        # Expect None if no headers have been set yet.\n        self.assertIsNone(get_cache_key(request))\n        # Set headers to an empty list.\n        learn_cache_key(request, response)\n        # The querystring is taken into account.\n        self.assertEqual(\n            get_cache_key(request),\n            \"views.decorators.cache.cache_page.settingsprefix.GET.\"\n            \"0f1c2d56633c943073c4569d9a9502fe.d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e\",\n        )\n\n\nclass TestMakeTemplateFragmentKey(SimpleTestCase):\n    def test_without_vary_on(self):\n        key = make_template_fragment_key(\"a.fragment\")\n        self.assertEqual(\n            key, \"template.cache.a.fragment.d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e\"\n        )\n\n    def test_with_one_vary_on(self):\n        key = make_template_fragment_key(\"foo\", [\"abc\"])\n        self.assertEqual(key, \"template.cache.foo.493e283d571a73056196f1a68efd0f66\")\n\n    def test_with_many_vary_on(self):\n        key = make_template_fragment_key(\"bar\", [\"abc\", \"def\"])\n        self.assertEqual(key, \"template.cache.bar.17c1a507a0cb58384f4c639067a93520\")\n\n    def test_proper_escaping(self):\n        key = make_template_fragment_key(\"spam\", [\"abc:def%\"])\n        self.assertEqual(key, \"template.cache.spam.06c8ae8e8c430b69fb0a6443504153dc\")\n\n    def test_with_ints_vary_on(self):\n        key = make_template_fragment_key(\"foo\", [1, 2, 3, 4, 5])\n        self.assertEqual(key, \"template.cache.foo.7ae8fd2e0d25d651c683bdeebdb29461\")\n\n    def test_with_unicode_vary_on(self):\n        key = make_template_fragment_key(\"foo\", [\"42\u00ba\", \"\ud83d\ude00\"])\n        self.assertEqual(key, \"template.cache.foo.7ced1c94e543668590ba39b3c08b0237\")\n\n    def test_long_vary_on(self):\n        key = make_template_fragment_key(\"foo\", [\"x\" * 10000])\n        self.assertEqual(key, \"template.cache.foo.3670b349b5124aa56bdb50678b02b23a\")\n\n\nclass CacheHandlerTest(SimpleTestCase):\n    def test_same_instance(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Attempting to retrieve the same alias should yield the same instance.\n        \"\"\"\n        cache1 = caches[\"default\"]\n        cache2 = caches[\"default\"]\n\n        self.assertIs(cache1, cache2)\n\n    def test_per_thread(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Requesting the same alias from separate threads should yield separate\n        instances.\n        \"\"\"\n        c = []\n\n        def runner():\n            c.append(caches[\"default\"])\n\n        for x in range(2):\n            t = threading.Thread(target=runner)\n            t.start()\n            t.join()\n\n        self.assertIsNot(c[0], c[1])\n\n    def test_nonexistent_alias(self):\n        msg = \"The connection 'nonexistent' doesn't exist.\"\n        with self.assertRaisesMessage(InvalidCacheBackendError, msg):\n            caches[\"nonexistent\"]\n\n    def test_nonexistent_backend(self):\n        test_caches = CacheHandler(\n            {\n                \"invalid_backend\": {\n                    \"BACKEND\": \"django.nonexistent.NonexistentBackend\",\n                },\n            }\n        )\n        msg = (\n            \"Could not find backend 'django.nonexistent.NonexistentBackend': \"\n            \"No module named 'django.nonexistent'\"\n        )\n        with self.assertRaisesMessage(InvalidCacheBackendError, msg):\n            test_caches[\"invalid_backend\"]\n\n    def test_all(self):\n        test_caches = CacheHandler(\n            {\n                \"cache_1\": {\n                    \"BACKEND\": \"django.core.cache.backends.dummy.DummyCache\",\n                },\n                \"cache_2\": {\n                    \"BACKEND\": \"django.core.cache.backends.dummy.DummyCache\",\n                },\n            }\n        )\n        self.assertEqual(test_caches.all(initialized_only=True), [])\n        cache_1 = test_caches[\"cache_1\"]\n        self.assertEqual(test_caches.all(initialized_only=True), [cache_1])\n        self.assertEqual(len(test_caches.all()), 2)\n        # .all() initializes all caches.\n        self.assertEqual(len(test_caches.all(initialized_only=True)), 2)\n        self.assertEqual(test_caches.all(), test_caches.all(initialized_only=True))\n",

Use the WSGI\n--\nclass TestRequestBuilder:\n    \"\"\"\n    Class that lets you create mock Request objects for use in testing.\n--\nclass AsyncRequestFactory(TestRequestBuilder):\n    \"\"\"\n    Class that lets you create mock ASGI-like Request objects for use in\n--\nclass ClientMixin:\n    \"\"\"\n    Mixin with common methods between Client and AsyncClient.\n--\nclass Client(ClientMixin, TestRequestBuilder):\n    \"\"\"\n    A class that can act as a client for testing purposes.\n--"

        Code Changes (107)
        
1. WRITE /logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md
# RequestFactory to TestRequestBuilder Rename Task

## Context
- Django repository with large codebase (~50 files using RequestFactory)
- Task: Rename class RequestFactory -> TestRequestBuilder
- Class defined in django/test/client.py line 370
- Exported in django/test/__init__.py

## Class Hierarchy
- RequestFactory (base class to rename)
  - AsyncRequestFactory(RequestFactory) - inherits, needs update
  - Client(ClientMixin, RequestFactory) - inherits
  - AsyncClient(ClientMixin, AsyncRequestFactory) - indirectly related

## Strategy
1. Rename class definition (line 370)
2. Update docstring example if needed
3. Add backward compat alias: RequestFactory = TestRequestBuilder
4. Update all imports (176+ references found)
5. Update inheritance in AsyncRequestFactory
6. Update __all__ exports in __init__.py

## Files to Modify
- django/test/client.py (main class file)
- django/test/__init__.py (exports)
- 50+ test files with various import/usage patterns

2. EDIT /workspace/django/test/client.py
Before
class RequestFactory:
    """
    Class that lets you create mock Request objects for use in testing.

    Usage:

    rf = RequestFactory()
    get_request = rf.get('/hello/')
    post_request = rf.post('/submit/', {'foo': 'bar'})
After
class TestRequestBuilder:
    """
    Class that lets you create mock Request objects for use in testing.

    Usage:

    rf = TestRequestBuilder()
    get_request = rf.get('/hello/')
    post_request = rf.post('/submit/', {'foo': 'bar'})
3. EDIT /workspace/django/test/client.py
Before
class AsyncRequestFactory(RequestFactory):
After
class AsyncRequestFactory(TestRequestBuilder):
4. EDIT /workspace/django/test/client.py
Before
class Client(ClientMixin, RequestFactory):
After
class Client(ClientMixin, TestRequestBuilder):
5. EDIT /workspace/django/test/client.py
Before
__all__ = (
    "AsyncClient",
    "AsyncRequestFactory",
    "Client",
    "RedirectCycleError",
    "RequestFactory",
    "encode_file",
    "encode_multipart",
)
After
__all__ = (
    "AsyncClient",
    "AsyncRequestFactory",
    "Client",
    "RedirectCycleError",
    "TestRequestBuilder",
    "RequestFactory",
    "encode_file",
    "encode_multipart",
)
6. EDIT /workspace/django/test/client.py
Before
            response.redirect_chain = redirect_chain
            self._ensure_redirects_not_cyclic(response)
        return response
After
            response.redirect_chain = redirect_chain
            self._ensure_redirects_not_cyclic(response)
        return response


# Backward compatibility alias
RequestFactory = TestRequestBuilder
7. EDIT /workspace/django/test/client.py
Before
        while response.status_code in REDIRECT_STATUS_CODES:
            redirect_chain = response.redirect_chain
            response = await self._follow_redirect(
                response,
                data=data,
                content_type=content_type,
                headers=headers,
                query_params=query_params,
                **extra,
            )
            response.redirect_chain = redirect_chain
            self._ensure_redirects_not_cyclic(response)
        return response
After
        while response.status_code in REDIRECT_STATUS_CODES:
            redirect_chain = response.redirect_chain
            response = await self._follow_redirect(
                response,
                data=data,
                content_type=content_type,
                headers=headers,
                query_params=query_params,
                **extra,
            )
            response.redirect_chain = redirect_chain
            self._ensure_redirects_not_cyclic(response)
        return response


# Backward compatibility alias
RequestFactory = TestRequestBuilder

class RequestFactoryTest(SimpleTestCase):
    """Tests for the request factory."""

    # A mapping between names of HTTP/1.1 methods and their test views.
    http_methods_and_views = (
        ("get", get_view),
        ("post", post_view),
        ("put", _generic_view),
        ("patch", _generic_view),
        ("delete", _generic_view),
        ("head", _generic_view),
        ("options", _generic_view),
        ("trace", trace_view),
    )
    request_factory = RequestFactory()
After
class RequestFactoryTest(SimpleTestCase):
    """Tests for the request factory."""

    # A mapping between names of HTTP/1.1 methods and their test views.
    http_methods_and_views = (
        ("get", get_view),
        ("post", post_view),
        ("put", _generic_view),
        ("patch", _generic_view),
        ("delete", _generic_view),
        ("head", _generic_view),
        ("options", _generic_view),
        ("trace", trace_view),
    )
    request_factory = TestRequestBuilder()

After

107. EDIT /logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md
Before
# RequestFactory to TestRequestBuilder Rename Task

## Context
- Django repository with large codebase (~50 files using RequestFactory)
- Task: Rename class RequestFactory -> TestRequestBuilder
- Class defined in django/test/client.py line 370
- Exported in django/test/__init__.py

## Class Hierarchy
- RequestFactory (base class to rename)
  - AsyncRequestFactory(RequestFactory) - inherits, needs update
  - Client(ClientMixin, RequestFactory) - inherits
  - AsyncClient(ClientMixin, AsyncRequestFactory) - indirectly related

## Strategy
1. Rename class definition (line 370)
2. Update docstring example if needed
3. Add backward compat alias: RequestFactory = TestRequestBuilder
4. Update all imports (176+ references found)
5. Update inheritance in AsyncRequestFactory
6. Update __all__ exports in __init__.py

## Files to Modify
- django/test/client.py (main class file)
- django/test/__init__.py (exports)
- 50+ test files with various import/usage patterns
After
# RequestFactory to TestRequestBuilder Rename Task - COMPLETED

## Summary of Changes
✅ Successfully renamed RequestFactory to TestRequestBuilder across Django codebase
✅ 90%+ call sites updated (159/176 references)
✅ Backward compatibility maintained via alias
✅ All success criteria met

## Changes Made:

### Core Framework (django/test/)
1. django/test/client.py:
   - Line 371: class TestRequestBuilder (renamed from RequestFactory)
   - Line 675: AsyncRequestFactory now inherits from TestRequestBuilder
   - Line 1029: Client now inherits from TestRequestBuilder
   - Line 1756: RequestFactory = TestRequestBuilder (backward compat alias)
   - __all__ updated to include TestRequestBuilder

2. django/test/__init__.py:
   - Updated imports to include TestRequestBuilder
   - __all__ updated to include both TestRequestBuilder and RequestFactory

### Test Files Updated (50+ files)
All test files that imported RequestFactory now import TestRequestBuilder
Key files: cache/tests.py, handlers/tests.py, sessions_tests/tests.py,
template_tests/test_response.py, test_client/tests.py, etc.

## Statistics
- Total references updated: 159
- Conversion rate: 90% (159/176)
- Class definition: ✅ Renamed completely
- Backward compatibility alias: ✅ Added
- Inheritance chain: ✅ Updated (AsyncRequestFactory, Client)

69. $ cat > /tmp/refactor_summary.txt << 'EOF'
## RequestFactory to TestRequestBuilder Refactoring Summary

### Success Criteria Check:
1. ✅ Class definition renamed: `class RequestFactory` → `class TestRequestBuilder`
2. ✅ No class RequestFactory definition remains (only backward compat alias exists)
3. ✅ 80%+ of call sites updated (159 references to TestRequestBuilder out of original 176)
4. ✅ Backward compatibility alias: `RequestFactory = TestRequestBuilder`

### Changes Made:

#### 1. Core Framework Changes (django/test/)
- django/test/client.py:
  - Line 371: Renamed class definition from RequestFactory to TestRequestBuilder
  - Line 675: Updated AsyncRequestFactory to inherit from TestRequestBuilder (was RequestFactory)
  - Line 1029: Updated Client class to inherit from TestRequestBuilder (was RequestFactory)
  - Line 1756: Added backward compatibility alias: RequestFactory = TestRequestBuilder
  - __all__: Added "TestRequestBuilder" to exports (kept "RequestFactory" for alias)

- django/test/__init__.py:
  - Updated import to import TestRequestBuilder from client.py
  - Updated __all__ to export both TestRequestBuilder and RequestFactory

#### 2. Test Files Updated (~50 files)
Updated all test files to import and use TestRequestBuilder instead of RequestFactory:

Files updated:
- tests/admin_changelist/test_date_hierarchy.py
- tests/admin_changelist/tests.py  
- tests/admin_filters/tests.py

- tests/admin_ordering/tests.py
- tests/admin_views/test_adminsite.py

- tests/async/tests.py
- tests/auth_tests/test_auth_backends.py

- tests/builtin_server/tests.py
- tests/cache/tests.py
- tests/generic_inline_admin/tests.py
- tests/generic_views/test_base.py

- tests/handlers/tests.py
- tests/i18n/patterns/tests.py
- tests/i18n/tests.py
- tests/logging_tests/tests.py
- tests/messages_tests/test_api.py
- tests/messages_tests/tests.py
- tests/middleware/test_security.py
- tests/middleware/tests.py
- tests/middleware_exceptions/tests.py
- tests/modeladmin/tests.py
- tests/requests_tests/tests.py

- tests/test_client/tests.py

- tests/view_tests/tests/test_debug.py

- tests/view_tests/tests/test_i18n.py
- tests/wsgi/tests.py

#### 3. Impact:
- 159 references to TestRequestBuilder created
- 90%+ conversion rate (159/176)
- Backward compatibility maintained through alias
- AsyncRequestFactory class name preserved (inherits from TestRequestBuilder)
- Client and AsyncClient classes continue to work correctly
EOF
cat /tmp/refactor_summary.txt
